<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

A little bit is normal, but fasciculations indicate extreme twitching.</div>"
"One lower motor neuron sign is <b>hypotonia</b>. What is this?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Skeletal&#x20;muscle&#x20;with&#x20;excessively&#x20;&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;style&#x3A;&#x20;italic&#x3B;&quot;&gt;weak&#x20;tone&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"One lower motor neuron sign is <b>hypotonia</b>. What is this?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Skeletal muscle with excessively <u style=""font-style: italic;"">weak tone</u></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

If we're instructed to totally relax our leg, a little bit of tone should still be present. For patients with hypotonia, this small amount of resistance would <i>not</i> be present.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Nociceptors&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp;&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;thermoreceptors&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;in the skin have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">bare nerve endings</b></span> - lack an ending structure on its axon.<br>"	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">Nociceptors</u></span> &amp;&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">thermoreceptors</u></span>&nbsp;in the skin have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">bare nerve endings</b></span> - lack an ending structure on its axon.<br>

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Dizygotic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[monozygotic/dizygotic]</span> <b>twins</b> are formed when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">two <i>different</i> sperm</span> fertilize <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">two <i>different</i> eggs</span> - both implanting in the uterus.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Dizygotic</b></span> <b>twins</b> are formed when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">two <i>different</i> sperm</span> fertilize <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">two <i>different</i> eggs</span> - both implanting in the uterus.</div>

How are&nbsp;<i><u>monozygotic&nbsp;</u></i><u>twins</u>&nbsp;formed?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Zygote&#x20;&#x28;1&#x20;egg&#x20;fertilized&#x20;by&#x20;1&#x20;sperm&#x29;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;splits&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;in&#x20;two&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;&#x2E;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Twins can be either&nbsp;<i>dizygotic&nbsp;</i>or&nbsp;<i>monozygotic</i>. - Cell undergoes rapid divisions to go from 1 cell → 8 cells.</div>"

"After fertilization, cleavage takes place. What is <b><i>indeterminant </i>cleavage</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Cleavage&#x20;of&#x20;cells&#x20;that&#x20;can&#x20;&lt;i&gt;still&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;differentiate&#x20;into&#x20;&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;style&#x3A;&#x20;italic&#x3B;&quot;&gt;whatever&#x20;cell&#x20;type&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;it&#x20;wants&lt;i&gt;&#x2E;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"After fertilization, cleavage takes place. <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>Monozygotic </b>twins result from this type of cleavage.</div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

// Prevent original image from loading before mask

    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}

    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}

}

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

"<b style="""">Leukocytes</b>&nbsp;use <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;diapedesis&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[process]</span> to squeeze <i>between</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">endothelial cells</span> to reach sites of inflammation."	"<b style="""">Leukocytes</b>&nbsp;use <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">diapedesis</u></span> to squeeze <i>between</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">endothelial cells</span> to reach sites of inflammation.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Leukocyte = <i>generic name</i> for <u><i>white blood cells</i></u></div><div><br></div><img src=""main-qimg-82e7c9c22f12fa26514d483cbd97d2f5.png""><br></div>"
"<b style="""">Leukocytes</b>&nbsp;use <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">diapedesis</u></span> to squeeze <i>between</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""endothelial&#x20;cells"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> to reach sites of inflammation."	"<b style="""">Leukocytes</b>&nbsp;use <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">diapedesis</u></span> to squeeze <i>between</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">endothelial cells</span> to reach sites of inflammation.

<div><img src=""Perforin-Granzyme-Apoptosis-Pathway-3.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Suppressor T cells</b></span> release <u style="""">cytokines</u> that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inactivate&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;other&#x20;leukocytes"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in order to <i>prevent </i>overactivation of the immune system."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Suppressor T cells</b></span> release <u style="""">cytokines</u> that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inactivate </i>other leukocytes</span> in order to <i>prevent </i>overactivation of the immune system.

<div>By contrast, T-lymphocytes mature in the <b>thymus</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""0caaf09c6b0f98806bd5207f9995a0cd.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;T&lt;sub&gt;h&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;2&#x20;CD4&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;cells&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[Th<sub>1</sub>/Th<sub>2</sub>]</span> induce <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">B-lymphocytes</span> to differentiate into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">plasma cells</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">memory B cells</u></span>." [sound:hypertts-a7720889a04fabdc10f1fca460a7ef8fc7b4e1332b2aa341e94036e7.mp3]"	"B-lymphocytes (humoral) <i><u>require</u> </i>Helpter T cells (cell-mediated) to <u><i>differentiate</i></u> into plasma cells &amp; memory B-cells. "<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Gut-associated lymphoid tissues (GALT)</b></span> are lymph tissues that protect the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>entire</i>&nbsp;<u style="""">digestive system</u></span> from infection.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

"Another name for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;epitope&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">antigenic determinant</b></span> on a pathogen." "<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Isotype&#x20;switching&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when plasma cells <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">switch which antibody</u></span> they produce (IgM&nbsp;→ IgG) when stimulated by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>specific</i> cytokines</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Isotype switching</b></span> is when plasma cells <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;switch&#x20;which&#x20;antibody&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> they produce (IgM&nbsp;→ IgG) when stimulated by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>specific</i> cytokines</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"Explain what would happen if an <b><i>Rh-</i></b> mother gave birth to an <b><i>Rh+</i></b> child.
<br>
[sound:hypertts-089cd3f4a8befacb750fd73ee7d4fe12986fc7c96d8ca42553949574.mp3]"	"<div>Called <b><u>erythroblastosis fetalis</u></b></div><div><br></div>Since maternal-fetal blood does not mix, no problem would be posed. After giving birth, the blood <i>does </i>mix - the mother will develop <i><u>Rh+ antibodies</u></i>.<div><br></div><div><i>Second </i>pregnancy: Mother has another Rh+ child, which would get attacked by the mother's newly developed Rh+ antibodies (from the first pregnancy).</div>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Dendritic&#x20;cells&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> carry antigens <i>from</i> damaged tissues <i>to</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">lymph tissues</u></span> to activate B/T-lymphocytes." <div>Shows the antigen to B &amp; T-lymphocytes to activate them for the immune response.</div><div><br></div><img src=""mail-truck-blurry-630x420.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Dendritic&#x20;cells&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> travel over to lymph tissue to show <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">B &amp; T-lymphocytes</u></span> the antigen from infected tissues."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Dendritic cells</b></span> travel over to lymph tissue to show <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">B &amp; T-lymphocytes</u></span> the antigen from infected tissues.

<div>Activates B &amp; T-lymphocytes</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""mail-truck-blurry-630x420.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Dendritic cells</b></span> travel over to lymph tissue to show <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;B&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;T&#x2D;lymphocytes&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> the antigen from infected tissues."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Dendritic cells</b></span> travel over to lymph tissue to show <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">B &amp; T-lymphocytes</u></span> the antigen from infected tissues.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;CD4&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;sup&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[CD4/CD8]</span><b style="""">&nbsp;T-lymphocytes</b>&nbsp;mature&nbsp;into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">T<sub style="""">h</sub>1</span>&nbsp;&amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">T<sub style="""">h</sub>2</span> cells."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">CD4</b><sup style="""">+</sup></span><b style="""">&nbsp;T-lymphocytes</b>&nbsp;mature&nbsp;into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">T<sub style="""">h</sub>1</span>&nbsp;&amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">T<sub style="""">h</sub>2</span> cells.

"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">autoimmune</b></span> diseases, they're&nbsp;<i>unable</i> to recognize <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;self&#x2D;antigens&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> on normal cells &amp; attack the host's cells." "Peptide&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pre&#x2D;prohormones"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> are <u>cleaved</u> into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""prohormones"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ER lumen</b></span>." "The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;golgi&#x20;apparatus&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[organelle]</span> cleaves peptide prohormones into peptide hormones." "What does the <b style="""">golgi apparatus</b> do to peptide hormones? (x2 main functions)<div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">- Cleaves prohormones&nbsp;→&nbsp;hormones</span><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">- Adds covalent modifications (sugars)</span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Cortisol &amp; cortisone want high blood glucose levels.</div>"
"<div><b style="""">Cortisol </b>&amp; <b style="""">cortisone </b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decrease&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increase/decrease]</span> <u>inflammation</u>.</div>"	"<div><b style="""">Cortisol </b>&amp; <b style="""">cortisone </b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decrease</i></span> <u>inflammation</u>.</div>

...because inflammation is apart of the immune system (which it suppresses)</div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""034d5d741f61b15fee13274adeeef4f556ee0ea8.png""></div>"
"<div>Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;independent&#x20;assortment&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">inheritance of one gene</span> <u><i>does not</i> affect</u> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">inheritance of another</span>.</div>"	"<div>Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>independent assortment</b></span> says that the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">inheritance of one gene</span> <u><i>does not</i> affect</u> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">inheritance of another</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Says that we're equally as likely to contain 1 gene as we are to contain another.</div><div>- There are a few cases where this isn't true, like linked genes.</div><div>...key word is independent.</div><div><br></div><img src=""random-gene-assortment_med.jpeg""></div>"
"<u>Tetrad</u> chromosomes are <i>only </i>present in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;eiosis&#x20;1&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<u>Tetrad</u> chromosomes are <i>only </i>present in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>m</b><b style="""">eiosis 1</b></span>.

"<b style="""">Anaphase </b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">1</b></span> separates <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>homologous&nbsp;</u><u style="""">tetrads</u></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""nondisjunction-2_med.jpeg""></div>"
"<div>Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;segregation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says the two alleles for each gene <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>separate</i> during meiosis</u>&nbsp;into different gametes</span>, where the expression of one allele <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>silences</i> the expression</span> of the other.</div>"	"<div>Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>segregation</b></span> says the two alleles for each gene <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>separate</i> during meiosis</u>&nbsp;into different gametes</span>, where the expression of one allele <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>silences</i> the expression</span> of the other.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- ""Silencing the expression of the other"" refers to Mendel's belief that every gene was expressed with complete dominance (not true).</div><div>- Alleles <i><u>randomly segregate (split apart)</u></i> into their own gametes.</div><div><br></div><img src=""nondisjunction-2_med.jpeg""></div>"
"Mendel's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">first</u></span> law is the law of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;segregation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"Mendel's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">first</u></span> law is the law of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">segregation</b></span>.

"The two components of the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;testes&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>seminiferous tubules</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>interstitial cells of Leydig</u></span>."	"The two components of the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>testes</b></span> are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>seminiferous tubules</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>interstitial cells of Leydig</u></span>.

<div>Semineferous tubules: produce sperm.</div><div>Interstitial cells of Leydig: produce testosterone.</div><div><br></div><img src=""spermatogenesis-human-anatomy.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""fd4c423b78cdbe70fc0742de0184ba16.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Semineferous tubules</b></span> <i>produce</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>sperm</u></span> in the testes.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Semineferous tubules</b></span> <i>produce</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>sperm</u></span> in the testes.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Sertoli&#x20;cells&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cells]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>nourish</i> developing sperm&nbsp;cells</span>&nbsp;in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>semineferous tubules</u></span> during spermatogenesis.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Sertoli cells</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>nourish</i> developing sperm&nbsp;cells</span>&nbsp;in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>semineferous tubules</u></span> during spermatogenesis.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- These <u>do not</u> mature sperm cells... they only aid in the process of spermatogenesis.</div><div>- Sertoli cells also <u>convert testosterone</u> (produced by the interstitial cells of Leydig) into dihydroxytestosterone (DHT).</div><div><br></div><img src=""5653f4d940120ae42811f314891a14ef.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Sertoli cells</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;nourish&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;developing&#x20;sperm&amp;nbsp&#x3B;cells"" data-ordinal=""2"">[function]</span>&nbsp;in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>semineferous tubules</u></span> during spermatogenesis.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Sertoli cells</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>nourish</i> developing sperm&nbsp;cells</span>&nbsp;in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>semineferous tubules</u></span> during spermatogenesis.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- These <u>do not</u> mature sperm cells... they only aid in the process of spermatogenesis.</div><div>- Sertoli cells also <u>convert testosterone</u> (produced by the interstitial cells of Leydig) into dihydroxytestosterone (DHT).</div><div><br></div><img src=""5653f4d940120ae42811f314891a14ef.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Interstitial cells of Leydig</b></span> <i>produce</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;testosterone&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span> in the gonads."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Interstitial cells of Leydig</b></span> <i>produce</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">testosterone</u></span> in the gonads.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><u>Sertoli cells</u> modify this testosterone by turning it into DHT (dihydroxytestosterone).</div><div><br></div><img src=""image_thumb1.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Interstitial&#x20;cells&#x20;of&#x20;Leydig&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>produce</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">testosterone</u></span> in the gonads."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Interstitial cells of Leydig</b></span> <i>produce</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">testosterone</u></span> in the gonads.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><u>Sertoli cells</u> modify this testosterone by turning it into DHT (dihydroxytestosterone).</div><div><br></div><img src=""image_thumb1.png""></div>"
"The scrotum is at a temperature <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2&#xB0;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;to&#x20;4&#xB0;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;lower than the rest of the body."	"The scrotum is at a temperature <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2°&nbsp;to 4°</span>&nbsp;lower than the rest of the body.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The <b>menstrual cycle</b> is trigged by low levels of estrogen &amp; progesterone (hence why FSH &amp; LH production increases).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9154d3f6080f94150eca0ba38bca7b36.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">menstrual cycle</span></span> is the cyclic <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>growing </i>&amp;<i> shedding</i></span> of the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;endometrium&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">menstrual cycle</span></span> is the cyclic <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>growing </i>&amp;<i> shedding</i></span> of the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>endometrium</u></span>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">GnRH</b></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">FSH</b></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">LH</b></span> secretions are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>high</i></span>&nbsp;when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>estrogen</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>progesterone</u></span> are <i>low</i>.

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">decidua</b></span> is a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">mucous membrane</span> that <i>lines</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>endometrium</u></span> during pregnancy - functioning to both <i>protect</i> the fetus from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">mother's immune system</span> &amp; to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">facilitate gas-exchange</span>.

"When <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;estrogen&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span> is at its highest, it<i style="""">&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>positively</i></span> feedsback on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>GnRH</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>LH</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>FSH</u></span> - causing spikes in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">LH</span> to trigger <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">ovulation</span>."	"When <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>estrogen</b></span> is at its highest, it<i style="""">&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>positively</i></span> feedsback on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>GnRH</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>LH</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>FSH</u></span> - causing spikes in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">LH</span> to trigger <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">ovulation</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...happens at the very end of the <u><i>follicular phase</i></u>.</div><div>- Normally estrogen negatively feedsback on those hormones.</div><div>- Estrogen's positive feedback causes FSH, LH, and GnRH levels to rise - resulting in the spiking LH seen that causes <b>ovulation</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9154d3f6080f94150eca0ba38bca7b36.png""><br></div></div>"
"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>estrogen</b></span> is at its highest, it<i style="""">&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;positively&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/-]</span> feedsback on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>GnRH</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>LH</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>FSH</u></span> - causing spikes in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">LH</span> to trigger <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">ovulation</span>."	"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>estrogen</b></span> is at its highest, it<i style="""">&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>positively</i></span> feedsback on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>GnRH</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>LH</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>FSH</u></span> - causing spikes in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">LH</span> to trigger <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">ovulation</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Estrogen's highest peak is right before ovulation, but it's still high towards the end fo the luteal phase.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9154d3f6080f94150eca0ba38bca7b36.png""><br></div></div>"

<div>- Estrogen's highest peak is right before ovulation, but it's still high towards the end fo the luteal phase.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9154d3f6080f94150eca0ba38bca7b36.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Contraceptive pills</b> are <i>high </i>doses of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;estrogen&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;progesterone&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span>, which <i>inhibit </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">FSH</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">LH</span> - stopping endometrium maintenance.</div>"	"<div><b>Contraceptive pills</b> are <i>high </i>doses of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>estrogen</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>progesterone</u></span>, which <i>inhibit </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">FSH</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">LH</span> - stopping endometrium maintenance.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Pills of&nbsp;<u>estrogen</u>&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;<u>progesterone</u>, which&nbsp;<i>negatively&nbsp;</i>feedback on GnRH, FSH, &amp; LH. FSH &amp; LH are no longer able to maintain the endometrium, so it gets <b>shed </b>and <b>prevents implantation.</b><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9154d3f6080f94150eca0ba38bca7b36.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;hCG&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span> <i>maintains</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">uterine lining</u></span> after the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">blastocyst</span> implants in the endometrium - preventing spontaneous miscarraige."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">hCG</b></span> <i>maintains</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">uterine lining</u></span> after the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">blastocyst</span> implants in the endometrium - preventing spontaneous miscarraige.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Remember, <b>LH </b>stimulates <b><u>progesterone</u></b> release from the corpus luteum.</div><div>- Progesterone is resposible for maintaining the endometrium.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9154d3f6080f94150eca0ba38bca7b36.png""><br></div></div>"

"Why is <b>hCG<i> </i></b>important during the first 1<sup style="""">st</sup> trimester of pregnancy?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""hCG&#x20;acts&#x20;as&#x20;a&#x20;&quot;fake&quot;&#x20;LH&#x2C;&#x20;which&#x20;binds&#x20;to&#x20;its&#x20;receptor&#x20;and&#x20;induces&#x20;&lt;u&gt;progesterone&#x20;secretion&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2E;&#x20;Progesterone&#x20;then&#x20;maintains&#x20;the&#x20;uterine&#x20;lining&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Why is <b>hCG<i> </i></b>important during the first 1<sup style="""">st</sup> trimester of pregnancy?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">hCG acts as a ""fake"" LH, which binds to its receptor and induces <u>progesterone secretion</u>. Progesterone then maintains the uterine lining.</span></div>

<div>...because volume increased upon expansion</div><div><br></div><img src=""7f6aad4dea2317b7285e6f166414838e.png""></div>"
"The space between the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""visceral"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""parietal"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> pleura is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">intrapleural space</b></span>." "Explain how <b style="""">negative-pressure breathing</b> is used to <i>suck in</i> air.

[sound:hypertts-9944295318c4942118d7359c8baf293d0b94f4cea5c810b7599c7d7a.mp3]"	"<div>As our diaphragm &amp; external intercostals contract, our lungs expand in size. Because the <i>volume of the lungs is now larger</i>, <i><u><b>lung pressure is lower</b></u></i>. The <i><u><b>pressure difference</b></u></i> <i>forces </i>air from a source of <u>high pressure</u> (outside) into the area with <u>lower pressure</u> (inside lungs).</div><div><br></div><img src=""7f6aad4dea2317b7285e6f166414838e.png"">

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

First line of defense</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Lysozyme</b></span>&nbsp;breaks down the <u style="""">peptidoglycan</u> of gram-<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span> bacterial walls." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

In gram-positive bacteria, the peptidoglycan layer is ""easier to access"" because it lacks the outer membrane.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Lysozyme&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[enzyme]</span>&nbsp;breaks down the <u style="""">peptidoglycan</u> of gram-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positive</i></span> bacterial walls."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Lysozyme</b></span>&nbsp;breaks down the <u style="""">peptidoglycan</u> of gram-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positive</i></span> bacterial walls.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

In gram-positive bacteria, the peptidoglycan layer is ""easier to access"" because it lacks the outer membrane.</div>"
"Cilia's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;mucociliary&#x20;escalator&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mechanism]</span> carries pathogens <i>up </i>the <u>respiratory tract</u> to the oral cavity."	"Cilia's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">mucociliary escalator</span></span> carries pathogens <i>up </i>the <u>respiratory tract</u> to the oral cavity.

"When the blood becomes <u>too basic</u>, why do respiratory rates <u><i>decrease</i></u>?
<br>
[sound:hypertts-ac93d11075ffa49f46fc9a29af622d488f7566166cc0192213fb79c2.mp3]"	"Decreased respiration ""holds on"" to more CO<sub>2</sub>. This pushes the equilibrium of the bicarbonate buffer system to the right, which leads to more H<sup>+</sup> formation (thereby making the blood more acidic &amp; balancing pH).<div><br></div><div>CO<sub>2</sub>&nbsp;+ H<sub>2</sub>O ⇌ H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>&nbsp;⇌ H<sup>+</sup>&nbsp;+ HCO<sub>3</sub><sup>-</sup><br></div>

<div><b>A-band</b> includes the overlap of thick &amp; thin filaments.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""dd31115c91fdcbdca1a43368ef6fbc34.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"On a <i>contracting </i>sarcomere, only the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>A-band</b></span>'s size <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;remains&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;constant&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"On a <i>contracting </i>sarcomere, only the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>A-band</b></span>'s size <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>remains&nbsp;</u><i><u>constant</u></i></span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">Sarcomeres</u></span> <i>attach together</i> to form <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;myofibrils&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>." "In muscles, <u>action potentials</u> are <i>initiated</i> on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;sarcolemma&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> and <i>distributed</i> through the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">transverse tubules (T-tubules)</b></span>."	"In muscles, <u>action potentials</u> are <i>initiated</i> on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">sarcolemma</b></span> and <i>distributed</i> through the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">transverse tubules (T-tubules)</b></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The sarcolemma is the <i>membrane-covering</i>&nbsp;of the muscle cell.</div><div>- Voltage-gated channels are located on the sarcolemma.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""796de507dfa0f6649582b38492e3a9b7.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In muscles, <u>action potentials</u> are <i>initiated</i> on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">sarcolemma</b></span> and <i>distributed</i> through the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;transverse&#x20;tubules&#x20;&#x28;T&#x2D;tubules&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In muscles, <u>action potentials</u> are <i>initiated</i> on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">sarcolemma</b></span> and <i>distributed</i> through the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">transverse tubules (T-tubules)</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The sarcolemma is the <i>membrane-covering</i>&nbsp;of the muscle cell.</div><div>- Voltage-gated channels are located on the sarcolemma.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""796de507dfa0f6649582b38492e3a9b7.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Transverse tubules (T-tubules<u>)</u></b></span> carry action potentials to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;sarcoplasmic&#x20;reticulum&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in muscle fibers."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Transverse tubules (T-tubules<u>)</u></b></span> carry action potentials to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u></span> in muscle fibers.

<div id=""io-wrapper"">
  <div id=""io-overlay""><img src=""7dd13b9e803a45b4b00e4bfdadd1140c-oa-1-A.svg""></div>
  <div id=""io-original""><img src=""tmp4237vksq.png"" /></div>
</div>

<button id=""io-revl-btn"" onclick=""toggle();"">Toggle Masks</button>
<div id=""io-extra-wrapper"">
  <div id=""io-extra"">
    
    
    
    
  </div>
</div>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<div>- This is NOT the same as ATP hydrolysis. ATP hydrolysis had to have happened prior to this step. "What happens to Ca<sup>2+</sup> <i>after </i>the muscle contraction is finished?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Ca&lt;sup&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;taken&#x20;back&#x20;into&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;b&gt;sarcoplasmic&#x20;reticulum&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;through&#x20;active&#x20;transport&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"What happens to Ca<sup>2+</sup> <i>after </i>the muscle contraction is finished?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Ca<sup>2+</sup> is <u><i>taken back into</i></u> the <b>sarcoplasmic reticulum</b>&nbsp;through active transport.</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div><b style="""">Muscle </b>cells exhibit an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""all&#x2D;or&#x2D;nothing"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> response to stimuli - they either contract fully or not at all.</div>"	"<div><b style="""">Muscle </b>cells exhibit an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">all-or-nothing</span> response to stimuli - they either contract fully or not at all.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"Muscles control force production by controlling the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;number&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;of&#x20;motor&#x20;units&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> they recruit."	"Muscles control force production by controlling the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>number </i>of motor units</u></span> they recruit.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

...strong vs. weak contractions are determined by the number of motor units.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Small"" data-ordinal=""1"">[small/large]</span> motor units are recruited <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>before</i></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""large"" data-ordinal=""1"">[small/large]</span> motor units."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Small</span> motor units are recruited <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>before</i></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">large</span> motor units.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Otherwise the muscule response would be <u style="""">too strong</u>.</div>"
"<b style="""">Muscle </b><b>cells</b> exhibit <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""summation"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in the same way as <u>neurons</u><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>to generate action potentials." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><i style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">Extracellular</i>&nbsp;pathogens are <b><i><u>bacteria</u></i></b>, <b><i><u>fungi</u></i></b>, &amp; <b><i><u>parasites</u></i></b>, since these are all <i>exogenous </i>cells entering the body.</div><u><i><div><u><i><br></i></u></div>Intracellular</i></u> pathogens are <b><i><u>viruses</u></i></b>, since they can only exist inside <i>other </i>cells - they themselves are <i>not</i> cells.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">CD4<sup>+</sup></b></span>: binds to MHC <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">2</span> - fights against <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;extracellular&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> pathogens.<br><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">CD8<sup>+</sup></b></span>: binds to MHC <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">1</span> - fights against <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;intracellular&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> pathogens</div>"	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">CD4<sup>+</sup></b></span>: binds to MHC <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">2</span> - fights against <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">extracellular</u></span> pathogens.<br><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">CD8<sup>+</sup></b></span>: binds to MHC <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">1</span> - fights against <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">intracellular</u></span> pathogens</div>

<div>...released via <b><i><u>exocytosis</u></i></b></div><div><br></div><img src=""9c4e68b4151819030040c911a52a4246.png""><br></div>"

Passively diffuses out on its own.</div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Polysynaptic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> reflex arcs use <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>interneurons</b></span> to transmit signals between <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>afferent</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>efferent</i></span> neurons.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">Polysynaptic</u></span> reflex arcs use <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>interneurons</b></span> to transmit signals between <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>afferent</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>efferent</i></span> neurons.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Polysynaptic</b> reflexes instead use interneurons as ""intermediates""</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""sJjde8jFwItdZYVzwmQF4A.png""><br></div></div>"
"Sensory &amp; motor neurons require <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">interneurons</b></span> to comunicate in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;polysynaptic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> reflex arcs"	"Sensory &amp; motor neurons require <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">interneurons</b></span> to comunicate in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">polysynaptic</u></span> reflex arcs
<hr>

"After stepping on a nail, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;withdraw&#x20;reflex&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[reflex]</span> stimulates our foot to pull away in&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">polysynaptic</u></span> reflex arcs." "<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Kinesins</span></span> carry vesicle-bound neurotransmitters to the axon's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">terminal</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""nejmcibr1112481_f1.jpeg""><br></div></div>"
"<i>Why</i> would blocking <b><i>K<sup>+</sup> gated channels</i></b> lead to <u><i>over-stimulation</i></u> of the neuron?
<br>
[sound:hypertts-516c1dbc6e6acb37266a99898b30d67ad0a20a2fcc55d0703494c68b.mp3]"	"<div>Would <i>prevent </i>K<sup>+</sup> from <i>exiting </i>the cell after depolarization - <b><i><u>keeping it more positive</u></i></b>... it'd be <i>easier </i>to <i style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">surpass the threshold potential</i>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1-s2.0-S2211034813000084-gr7.jpg""><br></div>

<br>
[sound:hypertts-e741bfbb53cb7f8b2e02ffb2b87791a13ba0b87de584c1e9f08287b9.mp3]
"
"The neuron's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">resting membrane potential</u></span> is maintained (partly) through <i>passive </i>transport, like <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;K&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;leak&#x20;channels&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The neuron's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">resting membrane potential</u></span> is maintained (partly) through <i>passive </i>transport, like <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">K<sup>+</sup> leak channels</span></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Also maintained through <b>active transport</b> (Na<sup>+</sup>/K<sup>+</sup>-ATPase) &amp; <b>selective permeability</b> as well.<div><br></div><div><img src=""5c70dcf2dd59ba91baf438ac35ea8575.png""><br></div></div>"
"The neuron's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;resting&#x20;membrane&#x20;potential&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is maintained (partly) through <i>passive </i>transport, like <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">K<sup>+</sup> leak channels</span></span>."	"The neuron's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">resting membrane potential</u></span> is maintained (partly) through <i>passive </i>transport, like <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">K<sup>+</sup> leak channels</span></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Also maintained through <b>active transport</b> (Na<sup>+</sup>/K<sup>+</sup>-ATPase) &amp; <b>selective permeability</b> as well.<div><br></div><div><img src=""5c70dcf2dd59ba91baf438ac35ea8575.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In neurons, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>myelin</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreases]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>capacitance</u></span> by keeping positive &amp; negative charges separated - resulting in a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;larger&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[smaller/larger]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">voltage</span>.</div>"	"<div>In neurons, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>myelin</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreases</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>capacitance</u></span> by keeping positive &amp; negative charges separated - resulting in a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">voltage</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Recall that <b>capacitance (C)</b> is inversely related to the separation of charges (distance). Since myelin separates the charges from each other, the capacitance is decreased and voltage is increased.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7bf590021238fd8bde556e627ccdc4c192315e6b.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""main-qimg-f36432e1f3ffd0ba5c16818ccfca645d.webp""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;GABA&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>is the main<i> </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibitory</i></span> neurotransmitter."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">GABA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>is the main<i> </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibitory</i></span> neurotransmitter.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<u>Glycine</u> is an inhibitory neurotransmitter too.<div>- Alcohols are GABA agonists.</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">GABA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>is the main<i> </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inhibitory&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[excitatory/inhibitory]</span> neurotransmitter."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">GABA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>is the main<i> </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibitory</i></span> neurotransmitter.

<div id=""io-header""></div>

  <div id=""io-overlay""><img src=""2eff642b64524d40a65cd74490031d77-oa-1-Q.svg"" /></div>

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask

    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<div>- CO = HR x SV</div><div>- CO is also equivalent to the volumetric flow rate (Q) of the blood.<br><br></div><div><div><img src=""ccbef1455067ec640ae1fdfba2333a31.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-abeb0a283a5315f5bffd8d98968584e9b57b8b8b.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Cardiac output</b></span> represents the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;total&#x20;volume&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of blood pumped per minute."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Cardiac output</b></span> represents the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">total volume</u></span> of blood pumped per minute.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- CO = HR x SV</div><div>- CO is also equivalent to the volumetric flow rate (Q) of the blood.<br><br></div><div><div><img src=""ccbef1455067ec640ae1fdfba2333a31.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-abeb0a283a5315f5bffd8d98968584e9b57b8b8b.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>cardiac output (CO)</b></span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""CO&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;HR&#x20;x&#x20;SV"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>cardiac output (CO)</b></span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">CO = HR x SV</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>CO </b>= cardiac output; <b>HR</b> = heart rate; <b>SV</b> = stroke volume.</div><div>- Flow rate (Q) is also equivalent to cardiac output.</div></div>"
"The <b>atria<i> </i></b>&amp; <span style=""font-weight: bold;"">ventricles</span><i style=""font-weight: bold;""> </i>both<i style=""font-weight: bold;"">&nbsp;</i>pump <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;the&#x20;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[different/the same]</span>&nbsp;<u>volumes</u> of blood."	"The <b>atria<i> </i></b>&amp; <span style=""font-weight: bold;"">ventricles</span><i style=""font-weight: bold;""> </i>both<i style=""font-weight: bold;"">&nbsp;</i>pump <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>the same</i></span>&nbsp;<u>volumes</u> of blood.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"Average <b style="""">cardiac output</b> is approximately <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""5"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>&nbsp;liters&nbsp;of blood <i>per minute</i>." CO = SV x HR</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Myocardial infarction</b></span>&nbsp;is triggered by a lack of bloodflow through the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;coronary&#x20;arteries&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Myocardial infarction</b></span>&nbsp;is triggered by a lack of bloodflow through the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">coronary arteries</u></span>.

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">diaphysis</b></span> contains <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;yellow&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[red/yellow]</span> bone marrow, and the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">epiphysis</b></span>&nbsp;contains <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;red&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[red/yellow]</span> bone marrow."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">diaphysis</b></span> contains <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">yellow</u></span> bone marrow, and the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">epiphysis</b></span>&nbsp;contains <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">red</u></span> bone marrow.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Red </b>bone marrow: Hematopoietic stem cells</div><div><b>Yellow </b>bone marrow: Fat</div><div><br></div><img src=""04f059d1fc196e5a4133889ee2d484e5.png""></div>"
"Over time, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""red"" data-ordinal=""1"">[red/yellow]</span> bone marrow is <i style="""">replaced</i> with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""yellow"" data-ordinal=""1"">[red/yellow]</span> bone marrow."	"Over time, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">red</span> bone marrow is <i style="""">replaced</i> with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">yellow</span> bone marrow.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Red bone marrow</b>: contains hematopoietic stem cells (in the epiphysis of long bones).<br></div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Yellow bone marrow</b>: fat storage (diaphysis of long bones).</div><div><br></div><img src=""9d0f80013c43390c05668caed088d612.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""04f059d1fc196e5a4133889ee2d484e5.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Osteogenic</b></span> stem cells give rise to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;osteoblasts&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;chondroblasts&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Osteogenic</b></span> stem cells give rise to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">osteoblasts</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">chondroblasts</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Osteoblasts</b>: turn into osteocytes after getting trapped in the blood matrix.</div><div>- <b>Chondroblasts</b>: turn into chondrocytes in the chondrin matrix.</div><div><br></div><img src=""00e489e8aa9dc73468de867925a9950a.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Osteogenic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> stem cells give rise to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">osteoblasts</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">chondroblasts</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Osteogenic</b></span> stem cells give rise to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">osteoblasts</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">chondroblasts</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Osteoblasts</b>: turn into osteocytes after getting trapped in the blood matrix.</div><div>- <b>Chondroblasts</b>: turn into chondrocytes in the chondrin matrix.</div><div><br></div><img src=""00e489e8aa9dc73468de867925a9950a.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Osteoblasts&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <i>secrete</i> the proteins needed for the formation of the <u style="""">bone matrix</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Osteoblasts</b></span> <i>secrete</i> the proteins needed for the formation of the <u style="""">bone matrix</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>As the bone matrix forms, osteoblasts get trapped - becoming osteocytes.</div><div><br></div><img src=""19447fafdc29ec45ae215a319de9aa05.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Mesenchymal&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;tissue"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> differentiates into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>connective tissue</i></u></span> in developing embryos."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mesenchymal&nbsp;</b>tissue</span> differentiates into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>connective tissue</i></u></span> in developing embryos.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mesenchymal&nbsp;</b>tissue</span> differentiates into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;connective&#x20;tissue&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in developing embryos."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mesenchymal&nbsp;</b>tissue</span> differentiates into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>connective tissue</i></u></span> in developing embryos.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Calcitonin</b></span> <i>decreases</i> blood-Ca<sup>2+</sup> levels by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inhibiting&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[promoting/inhibiting]</span> the action of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;osteoclasts&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[osteoblasts/osteoclasts]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Calcitonin</b></span> <i>decreases</i> blood-Ca<sup>2+</sup> levels by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>inhibiting</i></span> the action of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>osteoclasts</u></span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Calcitonin</b> works to decrease blood-calcium levels.<div>- Only inhibits osteoclasts - has no effect on osteoblasts.</div></div>"
"<b>PTH</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreases]</span> <u style="""">osteoclast</u> activity."	"<b>PTH</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span> <u style="""">osteoclast</u> activity.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The largest drop happens across the arterioles - hence why the <b>arterioles</b> are the primary means of <u>blood pressure regulation</u>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-5b48699ed376c70c77186b5701447120ba5b7d1e.jpg""></div>"
"What's the equation for the <i>change </i>in <b>circulatory pressure (ΔP)</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ΔP&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;CO&#x20;x&#x20;VR"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"What's the equation for the <i>change </i>in <b>circulatory pressure (ΔP)</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ΔP = CO x VR</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>ΔP</b> = pressure difference;&nbsp;<b>CO</b>&nbsp;= cardiac output;&nbsp;<b>VR</b>&nbsp;= vascular resistance.<div>- Cardiac output is equal to the volumetric flow rate (Q) of the blood.</div><div><div><b><br></b></div><div><img src=""paste-90cfdb837d24f7fa6b28e25a3c96b408578fa70b.jpg""><b><br></b></div><div><b><br></b></div><div><img src=""paste-ed56ce4e38f262a22fd9878a1cdb136352a4bae9.jpg""><b><br></b></div></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Longer"" data-ordinal=""1"">[longer/shorter]</span> blood vessels have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">higher</i></span> <b>resistance</b>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Longer</span> blood vessels have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">higher</i></span> <b>resistance</b>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Shows how <b>R</b> is <u>directly</u> proportional to length and <u>inversely</u> proportional to <b>area</b>.</div><div>- <b>ρ</b> is the restivity (constant) of the material.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""23b9f01ddb2420248b918c569cd3b9f5.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""images (1)-73b63dba94541c44cac8ea729d6180084f7ed72d.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Longer</span> blood vessels have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[higher/lower]</span> <b>resistance</b>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Longer</span> blood vessels have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">higher</i></span> <b>resistance</b>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Shows how <b>R</b> is <u>directly</u> proportional to length and <u>inversely</u> proportional to <b>area</b>.</div><div>- <b>ρ</b> is the restivity (constant) of the material.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""23b9f01ddb2420248b918c569cd3b9f5.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""images (1)-73b63dba94541c44cac8ea729d6180084f7ed72d.jpg""></div>"

"The <u style="""">kidneys</u> <i>excrete</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;bicarbonate&amp;nbsp&#x3B;HCO&lt;sub&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;sup&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in the urine when the blood becomes too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>basic</i></span>."	"The <u style="""">kidneys</u> <i>excrete</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">bicarbonate&nbsp;HCO<sub style="""">3</sub><sup style="""">-</sup></b></span> in the urine when the blood becomes too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>basic</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Due to LeChatelier's principle, this shifts the reaction to the right - <u>favoring</u> <b>HCO<sub>3</sub></b> + <b>H<sup>+</sup></b> formation (making it more acidic).</div>- CO<sub>2</sub>&nbsp;+ H<sub>2</sub>O ⇌ H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>&nbsp;⇌ H<sup>+</sup>&nbsp;+ HCO<sub>3</sub><sup>-</sup><div><sup><br></sup></div><div><img src=""paste-67b30148ac521094d3fc3bc6d2ca1f12dfe06cda.jpg""><sup><br></sup></div></div>"
"The <u style="""">kidneys</u> <i>excrete</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">bicarbonate&nbsp;HCO<sub style="""">3</sub><sup style="""">-</sup></b></span> in the urine when the blood becomes too <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;basic&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[acidic/basic]</span>."	"The <u style="""">kidneys</u> <i>excrete</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">bicarbonate&nbsp;HCO<sub style="""">3</sub><sup style="""">-</sup></b></span> in the urine when the blood becomes too <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>basic</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Due to LeChatelier's principle, this shifts the reaction to the right - <u>favoring</u> <b>HCO<sub>3</sub></b> + <b>H<sup>+</sup></b> formation (making it more acidic).</div>- CO<sub>2</sub>&nbsp;+ H<sub>2</sub>O ⇌ H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>&nbsp;⇌ H<sup>+</sup>&nbsp;+ HCO<sub>3</sub><sup>-</sup><div><sup><br></sup></div><div><img src=""paste-67b30148ac521094d3fc3bc6d2ca1f12dfe06cda.jpg""><sup><br></sup></div></div>"
"<div><i>Why </i>do the kidneys excrete <b>bicarbonate (HCO<sub>3</sub><sup>-</sup>)</b> in the urine when the blood's pH becomes <i>too </i><u>basic</u>?</div>
<br>
[sound:hypertts-319eb0b8e0e443ba7348331bffcbf5341d073ba9aef18065a8b7d895.mp3]"	"<div>Shifts the bicarbonate buffer system to the <i>right</i>&nbsp;due to LaChetlier's principle &amp; <i>increases</i> HCO<sub style="""">3</sub><sup style="""">-</sup> and H<sup style="""">+</sup> concentrations - making pH normal.</div><div><br></div>CO<sub style="""">2</sub>&nbsp;+ H<sub style="""">2</sub>O ⇌ H<sub style="""">2</sub>CO<sub style="""">3</sub>&nbsp;⇌ H<sup style="""">+</sup>&nbsp;+ HCO<sub style="""">3</sub><sup style="""">-</sup>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Hydrostatic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>pressure</b> is the pressure exerted <i>against</i> the vessel walls." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""11dba45db7200ad363384165d3829efc.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;Osmotic&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hydrostatic/osmotic]</span> pressure <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>pulls</i></span>&nbsp;water back into the bloodstream - generated by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">proteins</span></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Osmotic</span></span> pressure <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>pulls</i></span>&nbsp;water back into the bloodstream - generated by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">proteins</span></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Also called <b>oncotic pressure</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""11dba45db7200ad363384165d3829efc.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Osmotic</span></span> pressure <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>pulls</i></span>&nbsp;water back into the bloodstream - generated by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;text&#x2D;decoration&#x2D;line&#x3A;&#x20;underline&#x3B;&quot;&gt;proteins&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[macro.]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Osmotic</span></span> pressure <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>pulls</i></span>&nbsp;water back into the bloodstream - generated by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">proteins</span></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Trypsin</b>: cleaves near the C-terminal of arginine &amp; lysine residues.</div><div>- <b>Chymotrypsin</b>: cleaves near the C-terminal of aromatic amino acids.</div><div>- <b>Carboxypeptidase</b>: cleaves the terminal peptide bond on the C-side.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b9319509c1d2c57ec2798288a41b5982.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Trypsinogen</b></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">chymotrypsinogen</b></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>pro</b><b style="""">carboxypeptidase</b></span> are the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;protein&#x2D;digesting&#x20;zymogens&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> apart of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">pancreatic juice</span>." <div>Cholymicrons then pass through the lymphatic system before returning to the bloodstream</div><div><br></div><img src=""4668bde3773a8cc7b21ae4571765bfa9.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a87760a0ca116c22e03674b84a14fb1c9c9ab036.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Microvilli&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> lining the small intestine contain lymphatic ducts called <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">lacteals</b></span> that&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>uptake&nbsp;</i>cholymicrons</span> into the lymphatic system."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">Microvilli</u></span> lining the small intestine contain lymphatic ducts called <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">lacteals</b></span> that&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>uptake&nbsp;</i>cholymicrons</span> into the lymphatic system.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Cholymicrons then pass through the lymphatic system before returning to the bloodstream</div><div><br></div><img src=""4668bde3773a8cc7b21ae4571765bfa9.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a87760a0ca116c22e03674b84a14fb1c9c9ab036.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<b style="""">Monosaccharides </b>&amp; <b style="""">amino acids</b> are absorbed in the small intestine via <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;secondary&#x20;2&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2A;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[1*/2*]</span> active transport &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;facilitated&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> diffusion."	"<b style="""">Monosaccharides </b>&amp; <b style="""">amino acids</b> are absorbed in the small intestine via <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">secondary 2</u>*</span> active transport &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">facilitated</u></span> diffusion.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...since their transport is coupled with Na<sup>+</sup> flow</div><div><br></div><img src=""dff9e6642907681437237dd392fce3d0.png""></div>"
"<div>In the small intestine, <b>triglycerides</b> are <i>hydrolyzed </i>into <u>2-monoacylglycerols</u> &amp; <u>free fatty acids</u> before being packaged into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cholymicrons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to <i>enter</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""lymphatic&#x20;system"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In the small intestine, <b>triglycerides</b> are <i>hydrolyzed </i>into <u>2-monoacylglycerols</u> &amp; <u>free fatty acids</u> before being packaged into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cholymicrons</span> to <i>enter</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">lymphatic system</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- These <b>cholymicrons</b> are packed with <u>long chain fatty acids</u>, <u>cholesterol</u>, and <u>fat-soluble vitamins</u>.</div><div>- Has to pass through the lymphatic system first before it can enter the rest of the body.</div><div><br></div><img src=""4760f154d594b8d45241d241e845a889.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Cholymicrons&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are first absorbed into the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">lymphatic system</u></span>&nbsp;<i>directly</i> from the lacteals in the small intestine."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Cholymicrons</b></span> are first absorbed into the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">lymphatic system</u></span>&nbsp;<i>directly</i> from the lacteals in the small intestine.

"<u>Vaso</u><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;constriction&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><b>afferent</b></i></span><b><i> </i>arteriole</b> leads to the release of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">aldosterone</span>." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Autolysis</b> is when cells kill themselves in response to an <u>injury</u>&nbsp;(not programmed like apoptosis).<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-377c21397f72d97715bbadaba723fdd17ab14422.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"What's the <i>difference </i>between <b>apoptosis</b> &amp; <b>autolysis</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Apoptosis</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""normally&#x20;programmed&lt;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;cell&#x20;death&#x20;&#x28;by&#x20;mitochondria&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><b>Autolysis</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cell&#x20;death&#x20;triggered&#x20;by&#x20;&lt;u&gt;injury&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&#x28;by&#x20;lysosomes&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between <b>apoptosis</b> &amp; <b>autolysis</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Apoptosis</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">normally programmed<i> </i>cell death (by mitochondria)</span>.</div><div><b>Autolysis</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cell death triggered by <u>injury</u>&nbsp;(by lysosomes)</span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- These are <b>held in place</b>.&nbsp;</div><div>- Made of many different proteins NOT apart of the cell cortex.</div><div><br></div><img src=""41580_2007_Article_BFnrm2197_Fig1_HTML.jpg""></div>"
"Mitotic <b>microtubules </b>are <i>anchored down</i> on their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;minus&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-ends to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>centrosomes</u></span>."	"Mitotic <b>microtubules </b>are <i>anchored down</i> on their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>minus</u></span>-ends to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>centrosomes</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- MTOCs can be either <b><u>centrosomes</u> </b>or <b><u>centrioles/basal bodies</u></b> (cilia/flagella).</div><div><br></div><div>- Free plus-end can easily polymerize/depolymerize itself quickly.</div><div><br></div><img src=""rFtHbxuAmGlszTXhwc3dYQ_b.png""></div>"
"Mitotic <b>microtubules </b>are <i>anchored down</i> on their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>minus</u></span>-ends to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;centrosomes&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"Mitotic <b>microtubules </b>are <i>anchored down</i> on their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>minus</u></span>-ends to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>centrosomes</u></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- MTOCs can be either <b><u>centrosomes</u> </b>or <b><u>centrioles/basal bodies</u></b> (cilia/flagella).</div><div><br></div><div>- Free plus-end can easily polymerize/depolymerize itself quickly.</div><div><br></div><img src=""rFtHbxuAmGlszTXhwc3dYQ_b.png""></div>"
"To make 1 <b>centriole</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""27"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <i>total</i> <u>microtubules</u> are required."	"To make 1 <b>centriole</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">27</span> <i>total</i> <u>microtubules</u> are required.
