"The cell walls of <b>gram</b>-<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> bacteria are made of&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>peptidoglycan</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lipoteichoic acid</u></span>."	"The cell walls of <b>gram</b>-<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>positive</b></span> bacteria are made of&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>peptidoglycan</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lipoteichoic acid</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Peptidoglycan</b>: gives structural support to the cell wall.</div><div>- <b>Lipoteichoic acid</b>: (unknown function) but can be detected by the immune system.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Gram-positive-vs-Gram-negative.jpg""></div></div>"
"The cell walls of <b>gram</b>-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>positive</b></span> bacteria are made of&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;peptidoglycan&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lipoteichoic&#x20;acid&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The cell walls of <b>gram</b>-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>positive</b></span> bacteria are made of&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>peptidoglycan</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lipoteichoic acid</u></span>.
<hr>

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Peptidoglycan&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in <b>gram</b>-<b>positive/negative</b> bacteria can <i>cross-link</i> together to give the cell wall <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">structural support</i></span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>Peptidoglycan</u></span> in <b>gram</b>-<b>positive/negative</b> bacteria can <i>cross-link</i> together to give the cell wall <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">structural support</i></span>.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Only gram-negative bacteria have outer membranes, which surround both their plasma membrane &amp; cell walls.</div><div><u><br></u></div><div><u>Gram-positive</u>...</div><div>- <b>Lipotechoic acid</b></div><div><u>Gram-negative</u>...</div><div>- <b>Peptidoglycan</b>: gives structural support to the cell wall.</div><div>- <b>Lipopolysaccharides</b>: triggers <u>severe</u> inflammatory responses by the immune system.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Gram-positive-vs-Gram-negative.jpg""></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">cell walls</span> of&nbsp;<b>gram</b>-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>negative</b></span> bacteria are made of<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;peptidoglycan&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">outer membranes</span> are made of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lipopolysaccharides&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Form of sexual reproduction.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1abd72c96d0f740846764947a1f1e16c57c00db.png""><br></div></div>"
"In conjugation, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;males&#x20;&#x28;donors&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[males/females]</span> require <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">sex factor</b></span> plamids in order to form the <b style="""">sex pilus</b>." "In conjugation, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">males (donors)</u></span> require <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;sex&#x20;factor&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> plamids in order to form the <b style="""">sex pilus</b>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>F (fertility) factor </b>is the best studied sex factor.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1abd72c96d0f740846764947a1f1e16c57c00db.png""><br></div></div>"
"In conjugation, male bacteria <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;duplicate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>sex factors</b></span> <i>before </i>donating them to the female."	"In conjugation, male bacteria <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>duplicate</u></i></span> their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>sex factors</b></span> <i>before </i>donating them to the female.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...converts the female from F<sup>-</sup> to <b>F<sup>+</sup></b> <u>after</u> receiving it.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1abd72c96d0f740846764947a1f1e16c57c00db.png""><br></div></div>"
"In conjugation, male bacteria <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>duplicate</u></i></span> their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;sex&#x20;factors&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>before </i>donating them to the female." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...converts the female from F<sup>-</sup> to <b>F<sup>+</sup></b> <u>after</u> receiving it.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1abd72c96d0f740846764947a1f1e16c57c00db.png""><br></div></div>"
"In conjugation, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;F&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;sup&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[F<sup style="""">+</sup>/F<sup style="""">-</sup>]</span> bacteria <i>become </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;F&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[F<sup>+</sup>/F<sup>-</sup>]</span> after receiving the <b>F factor</b> from donor males." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""d1abd72c96d0f740846764947a1f1e16c57c00db.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Conjugation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[recombination]</span> allows traits like <u>antibiotic resistance</u> &amp; <u>virulence factors</u> to <i>propogate</i>&nbsp;through bacterial colonies."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Conjugation</b></span> allows traits like <u>antibiotic resistance</u> &amp; <u>virulence factors</u> to <i>propogate</i>&nbsp;through bacterial colonies.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Traits are transferred via plamids from one bacteria to the next.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1abd72c96d0f740846764947a1f1e16c57c00db.png""><br></div></div>"
"<i>After </i>conjugation, the transferred plasmid will <i>integrate</i> into the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>bacterial genome</u></span> via <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;transformation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[recombination]</span>."	"<i>After </i>conjugation, the transferred plasmid will <i>integrate</i> into the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>bacterial genome</u></span> via <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>transformation</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...called <b>Hfr</b>&nbsp;(high frequency of recombination) bacteria.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1abd72c96d0f740846764947a1f1e16c57c00db.png""><br></div></div>"
"<i>After </i>conjugation, the transferred plasmid will <i>integrate</i> into the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;bacterial&#x20;genome&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> via <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>transformation</b></span>."	"<i>After </i>conjugation, the transferred plasmid will <i>integrate</i> into the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>bacterial genome</u></span> via <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>transformation</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...called <b>Hfr</b>&nbsp;(high frequency of recombination) bacteria.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1abd72c96d0f740846764947a1f1e16c57c00db.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Transduction&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[recombination]</span> occurs when<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">viruses&nbsp;<i>accidentally&nbsp;</i>integrate&nbsp;<u>host DNA</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spread it</i></span> to <i>other </i>bacteria."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Transduction</b></span> occurs when<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">viruses&nbsp;<i>accidentally&nbsp;</i>integrate&nbsp;<u>host DNA</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spread it</i></span> to <i>other </i>bacteria.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...thereby facilitating the transfer of one bacteria's genes to another.</div><div><br></div><div>- Requires <b>viruses </b>to act as <b>vectors</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""5baba2057b974ea286e131f3e8e6a62214d00eca.png""></div>"

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Growth hormone</b></span> induces pseudo-<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""diabetes"" data-ordinal=""2"">[disease]</span> by <i style="""">inhibiting</i> glucose uptake in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>non-growing</u></span> tissues during puberty.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Growth hormone</b></span> induces pseudo-<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">diabetes</span> by <i style="""">inhibiting</i> glucose uptake in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>non-growing</u></span> tissues during puberty.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Allows that glucose to be used in growing tissues, instead.</div>"

"Do <i>all </i><u>viruses</u> have&nbsp;<b>envelopes</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;some&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;are&#x20;composed&#x20;&lt;i&gt;only&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;of&#x20;the&#x20;capsid&#x20;&#x28;made&#x20;of&#x20;proteins&#x29;&#x2C;&#x20;while&#x20;others&#x20;have&#x20;&lt;i&gt;both&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;a&#x20;&lt;b&gt;capsid&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;&lt;b&gt;outer&#x20;envelope&#x20;&#x28;lipids&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Do <i>all </i><u>viruses</u> have&nbsp;<b>envelopes</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i><u>No</u></i></b> - <u>some</u> are composed <i>only </i>of the capsid (made of proteins), while others have <i>both </i>a <b>capsid</b> &amp; <b>outer envelope (lipids)</b>.</span></div>

"<div>A virus' <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;capsid&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[structure]</span> is made of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>proteins</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>phospholipids</u></span> and <i>surrounds</i> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">genetic material</span>.</div>"	"<div>A virus' <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>capsid</b></span> is made of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>proteins</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>phospholipids</u></span> and <i>surrounds</i> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">genetic material</span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Capsids can be further surrounded by an <b>envelope </b>(lipids), but <u>not all</u> viruses have that.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""4b41c18e505fab69f0a710cad4393321.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Viruses</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;obligate&#x20;intracellular&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> parasites since they <i>require </i>the host cell's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ribosomes</span> for reproduction.</div>"	"<div><b>Viruses</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">obligate intracellular</u></span> parasites since they <i>require </i>the host cell's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ribosomes</span> for reproduction.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Viruses <b>lack ribosomes</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""4b41c18e505fab69f0a710cad4393321.png""><br></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Enveloped viruses instead fuse with the membrane.</div><div><br></div><div>- Cell <u>mistakes</u> it as being a <b>nutrient</b></div><div><br></div><img src=""cf8eb719a6d67560f7f763c92f08eb60d2dba9c9.png""></div>"
"<i>Some</i> <b>bacteriophages </b>use their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;tail&#x20;fibers&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[structures]</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">enzymatically digest</i></span> bacterial <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">cell walls</span> for easier injection."	"<i>Some</i> <b>bacteriophages </b>use their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>tail fibers</u></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">enzymatically digest</i></span> bacterial <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">cell walls</span> for easier injection.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...on top of also using them to <b>identify </b>the right bacteria &amp; <b>anchor themselves</b> down.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""4b41c18e505fab69f0a710cad4393321.png""><br></div></div>"

<div>...on top of also using them to <b>identify </b>the right bacteria &amp; <b>anchor themselves</b> down.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""4b41c18e505fab69f0a710cad4393321.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>HIV viruses</b> use the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;CCR5&#x20;receptor&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[receptor]</span>&nbsp;to <i>gain entry</i> into the cell."	"<b>HIV viruses</b> use the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>CCR5 receptor</u></span>&nbsp;to <i>gain entry</i> into the cell.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Those that lack the CCR5 receptor are <u>immune</u> to HIV.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""hb_overbaugh_virology-page-properties-image.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Once a virus enters a host cell, host <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ribosomes&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are used to <u>translate</u> the <b>viral </b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;b&gt;capsids&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[structures]</span> for its genetic information to be <i>stored </i>in."	"Once a virus enters a host cell, host <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ribosomes</b></span> are used to <u>translate</u> the <b>viral </b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><b>capsids</b></i></span> for its genetic information to be <i>stored </i>in.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Replicated genetic material (for the progeny) are then packaged inside of these capids.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Schematic-representation-of-the-transcription-and-translation-machinery-in-a-uninfected.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Viral progeny <i>exiting </i>the cell through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>exocytosis</u></span> with the membrane is called <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;extrusion&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[process]</span>."	"Viral progeny <i>exiting </i>the cell through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>exocytosis</u></span> with the membrane is called <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>extrusion</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Called the <b><u>productive cycle</u></b> since viruses are allowed to continue replicating (without breaking the cell).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e99850fc57a9db83cb724903af5e775f.png""><br></div></div>"
"Viral progeny <i>exiting </i>the cell through <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;exocytosis&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> with the membrane is called <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>extrusion</b></span>."	"Viral progeny <i>exiting </i>the cell through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>exocytosis</u></span> with the membrane is called <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>extrusion</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Called the <b><u>productive cycle</u></b> since viruses are allowed to continue replicating (without breaking the cell).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e99850fc57a9db83cb724903af5e775f.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Bacteriophages&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[viruses]</span> <i>replicate </i>inside host cells using <i>either </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lytic</u></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lysogenic</u></span> life cycle."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Bacteriophages</b></span> <i>replicate </i>inside host cells using <i>either </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lytic</u></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lysogenic</u></span> life cycle.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Bacteriophages are viruses that only infect bacteria.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""36e2d163733c711a8e5895f3031ab5d4.png""><br></div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Bacteriophages are viruses that only infect bacteria.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""36e2d163733c711a8e5895f3031ab5d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;lytic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lytic/lysogenic]</span> life cycle, <u>bacteriophages</u><b>&nbsp;</b><i>destroy&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">the host's genome</span><i>&nbsp;</i>to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>hijack</i></span> its machinery - destroying the cell."	"In the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">lytic</b></span> life cycle, <u>bacteriophages</u><b>&nbsp;</b><i>destroy&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">the host's genome</span><i>&nbsp;</i>to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>hijack</i></span> its machinery - destroying the cell.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""36e2d163733c711a8e5895f3031ab5d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"""Virulent"" <b>bacteriophages </b>use the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lytic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lytic/lysogenic]</span> life cycle."	"""Virulent"" <b>bacteriophages </b>use the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lytic</u></span> life cycle.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Phages in the lytic cycle kill bacteria.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""36e2d163733c711a8e5895f3031ab5d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;lysogenic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lytic/lysogenic]</span> life cycle, bacteriophages<b style=""""> </b>use their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">prophages</b></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>integrate&nbsp;</i>into the host's genome</span> to replicate their genes."	"In the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">lysogenic</u></span> life cycle, bacteriophages<b style=""""> </b>use their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">prophages</b></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>integrate&nbsp;</i>into the host's genome</span> to replicate their genes.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Prophage</b>: bactriophage genetic material that invades host genome.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-22b9e80eae07617317a1465b92cc4c3d02f093d2.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""36e2d163733c711a8e5895f3031ab5d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">lysogenic</u></span> life cycle, bacteriophages<b style=""""> </b>use their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;prophages&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>integrate&nbsp;</i>into the host's genome</span> to replicate their genes."	"In the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">lysogenic</u></span> life cycle, bacteriophages<b style=""""> </b>use their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">prophages</b></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>integrate&nbsp;</i>into the host's genome</span> to replicate their genes.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Prophage</b>: bactriophage genetic material that invades host genome.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-22b9e80eae07617317a1465b92cc4c3d02f093d2.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""36e2d163733c711a8e5895f3031ab5d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Viruses </b><i>temporarily</i> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lysogenic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lytic/lysogenic]</span> life cycle pose <i>less </i>of a threat to the host cell."	"<b>Viruses </b><i>temporarily</i> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lysogenic</u></span> life cycle pose <i>less </i>of a threat to the host cell.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Can help <b>prevent <u>superinfection</u></b> while it's integrated (infection from multiple viruses at once).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""36e2d163733c711a8e5895f3031ab5d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lysogenic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lysogenic/lytic]</span> life cycle, <b>bacteriophages </b><i>eventually </i>release from the cell by <i>becoming </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lytic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lysogenic/lytic]</span>&nbsp;&amp; breaking it open."	"In the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lysogenic</u></span> life cycle, <b>bacteriophages </b><i>eventually </i>release from the cell by <i>becoming </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lytic</u></span>&nbsp;&amp; breaking it open.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Makes the switch to lytic when <u>stimulated</u> by the environment in some way.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""36e2d163733c711a8e5895f3031ab5d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Prions&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are infectious proteins that cause <i>other </i>proteins with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>α-helices</u></span> to <i>misfold </i>into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>β-sheets</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Prions</b></span> are infectious proteins that cause <i>other </i>proteins with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>α-helices</u></span> to <i>misfold </i>into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>β-sheets</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Aggregation</b> occurs when the transition to β-sheets first happens, since the hydrophobic residues get exposed to the solvent. Multiple β-sheets aggregate together due to hydrophobic interactions.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2dd2c826c03e6805f337983bdd86b6ea35fa1054.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Prions</b></span> are infectious proteins that cause <i>other </i>proteins with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;α&#x2D;helices&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[α/β]</span> to <i>misfold </i>into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;β&#x2D;sheets&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[α/β]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Prions</b></span> are infectious proteins that cause <i>other </i>proteins with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>α-helices</u></span> to <i>misfold </i>into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>β-sheets</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Aggregation</b> occurs when the transition to β-sheets first happens, since the hydrophobic residues get exposed to the solvent. Multiple β-sheets aggregate together due to hydrophobic interactions.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2dd2c826c03e6805f337983bdd86b6ea35fa1054.jpg""><br></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b><u>Humans</u></b> are chemoheterotrophs (we obtain energy by eating other organisms like plants or animals).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""500_F_190713288_rQDFcMiAsBsXrjjgv2OUyUVOk1xyIow0.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1aafc8e2bd88c261dd518ba94729c88f.png""><br></div></div>"

"<u>Fungi</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>chemoheterotrophs</b></span>, meaning they obtain energy by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">eating&nbsp;<i style="""">other </i>organisms</span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Digestive enzymes</span> released by <b>sperm </b>to pierce through the <u>oocyte</u> are <i>stored </i>in its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""acrosome"" data-ordinal=""1"">[structure]</span>." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Hence why the reaction where sperm cells pierce the oocyte is called the <b><u>acrosomal reaction</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6eec98716264cc6c86c3897adee7c71f.png""></div>"
"The <b>sperm's </b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""midpiece"" data-ordinal=""2"">[structure]</span> contains <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>mitochondria</u></span>,&nbsp;which are needed to generate the ATP needed to swim."	"The <b>sperm's </b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">midpiece</span> contains <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>mitochondria</u></span>,&nbsp;which are needed to generate the ATP needed to swim.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>M</u></b>itochondrial <u><b>m</b></u>idpiece.</div><div>- The <b>acrosome</b> instead contains <u>digestive enzymes</u> needed to pierce the oocyte's zona pellucida.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6eec98716264cc6c86c3897adee7c71f.png""></div>"
"While the <b>acrosomal reaction</b>&nbsp;takes place, the corona radiata <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;has&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[has/has not]</span> been shed."	"While the <b>acrosomal reaction</b>&nbsp;takes place, the corona radiata <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>has not</i></span> been shed.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Sperm cells just ""weave"" their way through it.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""905fbea3f7bac856d8494fee75e99efd.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"In the <b>acrosomal reaction</b>, sperm cells bind to receptors on the oocyte's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">zona pellucida</span> to trigger digestive-enzyme release.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Small cross-sectional areas (like capillaries) normally propel blood through at faster rates. However, if you look at the combined speed relative to every single individual capillary, they're on average <u>slow</u>&nbsp;due to their larger combined cross-sectional areas.</div><div><br></div><img src=""20bccebdcae028b751590379d616a751.png""></div>"

</div>"
"<b>2,3-BPG</b>, <b>CO<sub>2</sub></b>, and <b>H<sup>+</sup></b> stabilize <u>hemoglobin's</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""T"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-state."	"<b>2,3-BPG</b>, <b>CO<sub>2</sub></b>, and <b>H<sup>+</sup></b> stabilize <u>hemoglobin's</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">T</span>-state.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Stabilizing the T-state means O<sub>2</sub> has a harder time binding to hemoglobin - allowing it to remain in the plasma &amp; enter tissues.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Carbonic&#x20;anhydrase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> <i>speeds up</i> the <u>bicarbonate buffer system</u> by catalyzing <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub></span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">CO<sub>2</sub> + H<sub>2</sub>O</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Carbonic anhydrase</b></span> <i>speeds up</i> the <u>bicarbonate buffer system</u> by catalyzing <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub></span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">CO<sub>2</sub> + H<sub>2</sub>O</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Uses a <b>zinc</b> cofactor.</div><div>- Considered an <b>anhydrase</b> enzyme because it ""removes"" a water molecule from carbonic acid.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-67b30148ac521094d3fc3bc6d2ca1f12dfe06cda.jpg""><br></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Carbonic anhydrase</b></span> <i>speeds up</i> the <u>bicarbonate buffer system</u> by catalyzing <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""H&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;CO&lt;sub&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""CO&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;H&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;O"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Carbonic anhydrase</b></span> <i>speeds up</i> the <u>bicarbonate buffer system</u> by catalyzing <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub></span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">CO<sub>2</sub> + H<sub>2</sub>O</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>Nuclear export signals</b> are requried for proteins to exit the nucleus.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Proteins</span> require <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;nuclear&#x20;export&#x20;sequences&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[sequences]</span> to exit<i> </i>the <u>nucleus</u> <i>through</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""nuclear&#x20;pores"" data-ordinal=""1"">[structure]</span>." <b>Nuclear localization signals</b> are requried for proteins to enter the nucleus.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Reverse transcriptase</b></span>&nbsp;<i>first</i> creates&nbsp;a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""DNA&#x2D;RNA&#x20;hybrid"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, <i>degrades</i> the RNA, and then uses <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""DNA&#x20;polymerase"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to polymerize the <i>rest</i> of the dsDNA."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Reverse transcriptase</b></span>&nbsp;<i>first</i> creates&nbsp;a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">DNA-RNA hybrid</span>, <i>degrades</i> the RNA, and then uses <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">DNA polymerase</span> to polymerize the <i>rest</i> of the dsDNA.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Inactivated second X-chromosome is called a <b>Barr body</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""7afda686cfb9075a4f24d57e9f566db0.png""></div>"
"In females, the second <i>randomly inactivated </i>X-chromosome is called a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Barr&#x20;body"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In females, the second <i>randomly inactivated </i>X-chromosome is called a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Barr body</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These often result in <u>mosaic phenotypes</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""7afda686cfb9075a4f24d57e9f566db0.png""></div>"
"<b>Ribosomes </b>can synthesize proteins <i>either </i>in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cytosol"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> or on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""rough&#x20;ER"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Ribosomes </b>can synthesize proteins <i>either </i>in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cytosol</span> or on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">rough ER</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""54d8192ffe4e96920c4c616a4cb622be.png""></div>"
"<div>For ER-destined proteins, the mRNA transcript's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""signal&#x20;sequence"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is <i>translated </i>into a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""signal&#x20;peptide"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - attracting SRPs for guidance to the rough ER.</div>"	"<div>For ER-destined proteins, the mRNA transcript's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">signal sequence</span> is <i>translated </i>into a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">signal peptide</span> - attracting SRPs for guidance to the rough ER.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""54d8192ffe4e96920c4c616a4cb622be.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Signal&#x20;recognition&#x20;particles&#x20;&#x28;SRPs&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> detect <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ER</b> <b>signal peptides</b> on proteins</span>, which then <i>direct </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ribosomes</u></span>&nbsp;to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>rough ER</u></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Signal recognition particles (SRPs)</span> detect <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ER</b> <b>signal peptides</b> on proteins</span>, which then <i>direct </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ribosomes</u></span>&nbsp;to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>rough ER</u></span>.</div>

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">suprachiasmatic</b></span>&nbsp;<b style="""">nucleus&nbsp;</b>in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""hypothalamus"" data-ordinal=""3"">[brain region]</span> controls <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">sleep-wake cycles</u></span>." "When light levels are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>suprachiasmtic</b></span>&nbsp;<b>nucleus</b>&nbsp;in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">hypothalamus</span> <i>upregulates </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>melatonin</u></span> levels to regulate sleep-wake cycles." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""0b932394594f7b61eceba34c04688116.png""></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;complete&#x20;dominance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[pattern]</span> is when the dominant allele <i>completely masks</i> the expression of the recessive allele."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>complete dominance</b></span> is when the dominant allele <i>completely masks</i> the expression of the recessive allele.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""Dominates"" it.</div><div><br></div><img src=""FlowersDRIDC.jpg""><br></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;incomplete&amp;nbsp&#x3B;dominance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[pattern]</span> is when <i>both</i> alleles for a gene are<i>&nbsp;</i>expressed - creating a ""blended"" phenotype."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>incomplete&nbsp;dominance</b></span> is when <i>both</i> alleles for a gene are<i>&nbsp;</i>expressed - creating a ""blended"" phenotype.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""FlowersDRIDC.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;co&#x2D;dominance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[pattern]</span> is when both alleles are both <i>fully </i>expressed - resulting in a <u>mosaic phenotype</u>."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>co-dominance</b></span> is when both alleles are both <i>fully </i>expressed - resulting in a <u>mosaic phenotype</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""FlowersDRIDC.jpg""></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;penetrance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> refers to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>proportion </i>of individuals in a <u>population</u></span>&nbsp;carrying&nbsp;a particular allele that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>expresses&nbsp;</i>its phenotype</span>."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>penetrance</b></span> refers to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>proportion </i>of individuals in a <u>population</u></span>&nbsp;carrying&nbsp;a particular allele that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>expresses&nbsp;</i>its phenotype</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Huntington's disease example...</div><div>- More than 40 sequence repeats: full penetrance (100% express the Huntington's phenotype).</div><div>- Less than 40 sequence repeats: high penetrance (75% show phenotype, 25% are unaffected).</div><div>- Only a few repeats: low penetrance (majority do not show symptoms).</div><div><br></div><img src=""d3e9f734d60b65c585ad6aec6f4b4220.png""></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>penetrance</b></span> refers to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>proportion </i>of individuals in a <u>population</u></span>&nbsp;carrying&nbsp;a particular allele that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;expresses&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;its&#x20;phenotype"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>penetrance</b></span> refers to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>proportion </i>of individuals in a <u>population</u></span>&nbsp;carrying&nbsp;a particular allele that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>expresses&nbsp;</i>its phenotype</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Huntington's disease example...</div><div>- More than 40 sequence repeats: full penetrance (100% express the Huntington's phenotype).</div><div>- Less than 40 sequence repeats: high penetrance (75% show phenotype, 25% are unaffected).</div><div>- Only a few repeats: low penetrance (majority do not show symptoms).</div><div><br></div><img src=""d3e9f734d60b65c585ad6aec6f4b4220.png""></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;full&amp;nbsp&#x3B;penetrance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when 100% of people carrying a particular allele in a <u>population</u> <i>express</i> its phenotype."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>full&nbsp;penetrance</b></span> is when 100% of people carrying a particular allele in a <u>population</u> <i>express</i> its phenotype.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Huntington's disease example...</div><div>- More than 40 sequence repeats: full penetrance (100% express the Huntington's phenotype).</div><div>- Less than 40 sequence repeats: high penetrance (75% show phenotype, 25% are unaffected).</div><div>- Only a few repeats: low penetrance (majority do not show symptoms).</div><div><br></div><img src=""d3e9f734d60b65c585ad6aec6f4b4220.png""></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;high&#x20;penetrance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when <i>most </i>people carrying a particular allele in a <u>population</u> <i>express</i> its phenotype&nbsp;(but not all)."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>high penetrance</b></span> is when <i>most </i>people carrying a particular allele in a <u>population</u> <i>express</i> its phenotype&nbsp;(but not all).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Huntington's disease example...</div><div>- More than 40 sequence repeats: full penetrance (100% express the Huntington's phenotype).</div><div>- Less than 40 sequence repeats: high penetrance (75% show phenotype, 25% are unaffected).</div><div>- Only a few repeats: low penetrance (majority do not show symptoms).</div><div><br></div><img src=""d3e9f734d60b65c585ad6aec6f4b4220.png""></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;low&#x20;penetrance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when <i>most </i>people carrying a particular allele in a <u>population</u> <i>do not&nbsp;</i>express its phenotype."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>low penetrance</b></span> is when <i>most </i>people carrying a particular allele in a <u>population</u> <i>do not&nbsp;</i>express its phenotype.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Huntington's disease example...</div><div>- More than 40 sequence repeats: full penetrance (100% express the Huntington's phenotype).</div><div>- Less than 40 sequence repeats: high penetrance (75% show phenotype, 25% are unaffected).</div><div>- Only a few repeats: low penetrance (majority do not show symptoms).</div><div><br></div><img src=""d3e9f734d60b65c585ad6aec6f4b4220.png""></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;expressivity&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> refers to how people with <i>identical</i> genotypes might <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>express</i> their <u>phenotypes</u> to <i>different</i> degrees</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Alternatively, late endosomes can transfer it to the golgi to repackage it for exocytosis.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8db3370f4c54ec7af087daad6d9feffc.jpg""><br></div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Alternatively, late endosomes can transfer it to the golgi to repackage it for exocytosis.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8db3370f4c54ec7af087daad6d9feffc.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In the endocytotic pathway, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;late&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;endosomes&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> can transfer cargo to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>golgi apparatus</b></span> to re-package the cargo for exocytosis."	"In the endocytotic pathway, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>late </i>endosomes</u></span> can transfer cargo to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>golgi apparatus</b></span> to re-package the cargo for exocytosis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Alternatively, late endosomes can transfer it to the lysosome to destroy it.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8db3370f4c54ec7af087daad6d9feffc.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In the endocytotic pathway, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>late </i>endosomes</u></span> can transfer cargo to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;golgi&#x20;apparatus&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[organelle]</span> to re-package the cargo for exocytosis."	"In the endocytotic pathway, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>late </i>endosomes</u></span> can transfer cargo to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>golgi apparatus</b></span> to re-package the cargo for exocytosis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Alternatively, late endosomes can transfer it to the lysosome to destroy it.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8db3370f4c54ec7af087daad6d9feffc.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Proteins tagged with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ubiquitin&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are sent to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>proteasomes</u></span> for destruction." "<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Longer</span> <b>axons</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[lower/higher]</span> resistance &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;slower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[slower/faster]</span> <u>speeds</u> of conduction.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Longer</span> <b>axons</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> resistance &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>slower</i></span> <u>speeds</u> of conduction.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Remember, resistance (R) is directly proportional to length (R = ρL/area)</div><div>- Don't confuse length with diameter - thicker axons travel faster!<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-14e9dca69cde9a2ad2d02ef03a93c0bce42c6475.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""85b45b811b9ae08602dd0ba9ae61c63e.png""><br></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Reactive oxygen species (ROS) are usually produced in the mitochondria's ETC. Excess ROS can trigger apoptosis.</div><div>- Cytochrome C activates <b>caspase proteases</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8492aa484f1c8680a15bb14488f491cd2f0c2f8b.jpg""><div><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Cytochrome&#x20;C&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[protein]</span> entering the cell's cytoplasm triggers the <i>activation</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>caspase proteases</u></span>, which signal for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">apoptosis</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cytochrome C</b></span> entering the cell's cytoplasm triggers the <i>activation</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>caspase proteases</u></span>, which signal for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">apoptosis</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Cytochrome C arrives in the cytosol after the mitochondria permeabilizes itself in respose to ROS accumulation.</div><div>- Reactive oxygen species (ROS) are usually produced in the mitochondria's ETC. Excess ROS can trigger apoptosis.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8492aa484f1c8680a15bb14488f491cd2f0c2f8b.jpg""><div><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cytochrome C</b></span> entering the cell's cytoplasm triggers the <i>activation</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;caspase&#x20;proteases&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[enzymes]</span>, which signal for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""apoptosis"" data-ordinal=""2"">[pathway]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cytochrome C</b></span> entering the cell's cytoplasm triggers the <i>activation</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>caspase proteases</u></span>, which signal for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">apoptosis</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Cytochrome C arrives in the cytosol after the mitochondria permeabilizes itself in respose to ROS accumulation.</div><div>- Reactive oxygen species (ROS) are usually produced in the mitochondria's ETC. Excess ROS can trigger apoptosis.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8492aa484f1c8680a15bb14488f491cd2f0c2f8b.jpg""><div><br></div></div>"
"The ""lub"" sound corresponds to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""systole"" data-ordinal=""1"">[systole/diastole]</span>, and the ""dub"" sound corresponds to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""diastole"" data-ordinal=""1"">[systole/diastole]</span> in the heart."	"The ""lub"" sound corresponds to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">systole</span>, and the ""dub"" sound corresponds to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">diastole</span> in the heart.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- ""Lub"" (systole) → ventricles contract &amp; atrioventricular valves close.</div><div>- ""Dub"" (diastole) → atria contract &amp; semilunar values close.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""942b380c38bee21116bfd1af07445238.png""></div><div><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;lymphatic&#x20;system&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[organ system]</span> functions to carry fluids that have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>leaked into the interstitium</u></span> from blood vessels <i>back into</i> circulation."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>lymphatic system</b></span> functions to carry fluids that have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>leaked into the interstitium</u></span> from blood vessels <i>back into</i> circulation.

"<div>Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>segregation</b></span> says the two alleles for each gene <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;separate&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;during&#x20;meiosis&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;into&#x20;different&#x20;gametes"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, where the expression of one allele <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;silences&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;expression"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of the other.</div>"	"<div>Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>segregation</b></span> says the two alleles for each gene <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>separate</i> during meiosis</u>&nbsp;into different gametes</span>, where the expression of one allele <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>silences</i> the expression</span> of the other.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- ""Silencing the expression of the other"" refers to Mendel's belief that every gene was expressed with complete dominance (not true).</div><div>- Alleles <i><u>randomly segregate (split apart)</u></i> into their own gametes.</div><div><br></div><img src=""nondisjunction-2_med.jpeg""></div>"
"Mendel's law of <b>segregation</b> is exemplified in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""anaphase"" data-ordinal=""1"">[stage]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> of <u>meiosis</u>."	"Mendel's law of <b>segregation</b> is exemplified in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">anaphase</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span> of <u>meiosis</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Says alleles&nbsp;<u style="""">randomly segregate (split apart)</u>&nbsp;into their own gametes, and that the expression of one allele masks the expression of the other (not entirely true).</div><div><br></div><img src=""nondisjunction-2_med.jpeg""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Transposons&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are fragments of chromosomes capable of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>rearranging</i> themselves</u>&nbsp;within the genome</span> and disrupting gene activity."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Transposons</b></span> are fragments of chromosomes capable of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>rearranging</i> themselves</u>&nbsp;within the genome</span> and disrupting gene activity.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- If they insert or delete themselves in the middle of a coding gene, the activity of the coding gene can become disrupted.</div><div>- Transposons use <b>transposase</b> to gain their ""cut and paste"" ability.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c9df86653bad36578386f0588a953b50f6550918.jpg""><br></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Transposons</b></span> are fragments of chromosomes capable of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;rearranging&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;themselves&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;within&#x20;the&#x20;genome"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> and disrupting gene activity."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Transposons</b></span> are fragments of chromosomes capable of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>rearranging</i> themselves</u>&nbsp;within the genome</span> and disrupting gene activity.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- If they insert or delete themselves in the middle of a coding gene, the activity of the coding gene can become disrupted.</div><div>- Transposons use <b>transposase</b> to gain their ""cut and paste"" ability.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c9df86653bad36578386f0588a953b50f6550918.jpg""><br></div>"
"Individuals <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;hetero&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><u>zygous</u> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>sickle cell anemia</b></span> have the <i>best</i> outcomes, because it confers a selective advantage against the development of malaria."	"Individuals <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>hetero</u></span><u>zygous</u> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>sickle cell anemia</b></span> have the <i>best</i> outcomes, because it confers a selective advantage against the development of malaria.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Prevents the malaria plasmodium from being able to replicate due to the shortened lifespan of RBCs.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8bf156ec57aace311f8ee7c247cc5081bf2f1797.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0b75d02ffba1d30ddcad9d8d7ac026bdc2a39da3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Individuals <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>hetero</u></span><u>zygous</u> for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;sickle&#x20;cell&#x20;anemia&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[disease]</span> have the <i>best</i> outcomes, because it confers a selective advantage against the development of malaria."	"Individuals <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>hetero</u></span><u>zygous</u> for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>sickle cell anemia</b></span> have the <i>best</i> outcomes, because it confers a selective advantage against the development of malaria.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Hybrid</b> offspring result.</div><div>- Ex: <b>mules</b> are formed from the cross between a male horse &amp; female donkey.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce158f8940eebd5bd3aa560ddc3b9e030ee5f735.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Hybrid offspring</b> (resulting from the mating of organisms from 2 <i>different</i> species) have an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""odd"" data-ordinal=""1"">[even/odd]</span> number of chromosomes - <i>preventing</i> them from mating."	"<b>Hybrid offspring</b> (resulting from the mating of organisms from 2 <i>different</i> species) have an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">odd</span> number of chromosomes - <i>preventing</i> them from mating.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Organisms with an odd number of chromosomes are unable to reproduce since they're unable to undergo proper homologous pairing to form gametes.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce158f8940eebd5bd3aa560ddc3b9e030ee5f735.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Genetic&#x20;drift&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> occurs when the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">number of alleles</span> for a certain trait in a population <i>changes</i> due to <u>random chance</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Genetic drift</b></span> occurs when the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">number of alleles</span> for a certain trait in a population <i>changes</i> due to <u>random chance</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Conversely, the <b>genotypic</b> ratio is 1:2:1.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2c1ac928fefcc08434c929f901656dca294c836b.jpg""></div>"
"Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>independent assortment</b></span> <i>only</i> holds true when the genes are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;non&#x2D;linked&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x28;&lt;i&gt;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;on&#x20;the&#x20;same&#x20;chromosome&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>independent assortment</b></span> <i>only</i> holds true when the genes are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>non-linked</u> (<i>not</i> on the same chromosome)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Says that we're equally as likely to contain 1 gene as we are to contain another.</div><div>...key word is independent.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""034d5d741f61b15fee13274adeeef4f556ee0ea8.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""random-gene-assortment_med.jpeg""><br></div>"
"Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;independent&#x20;assortment&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>only</i> holds true when the genes are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>non-linked</u> (<i>not</i> on the same chromosome)</span>."	"Mendel's law of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>independent assortment</b></span> <i>only</i> holds true when the genes are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>non-linked</u> (<i>not</i> on the same chromosome)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Says that we're equally as likely to contain 1 gene as we are to contain another.</div><div>...key word is independent.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""034d5d741f61b15fee13274adeeef4f556ee0ea8.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""random-gene-assortment_med.jpeg""><br></div>"
"In a dihybrid cross with complete dominance, two <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>hetero</u><u>zygous</u> <u>(</u>PpTt x PpTt<u>)</u></span></u>&nbsp;parents produce offspring with a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""9"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>:<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>:<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>:<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>&nbsp;ratio of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>phenotypes</b></span>."	"In a dihybrid cross with complete dominance, two <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>hetero</u><u>zygous</u> <u>(</u>PpTt x PpTt<u>)</u></span></u>&nbsp;parents produce offspring with a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">9</span>:<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;ratio of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>phenotypes</b></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 9 → dominant phenotypes for both traits.</div><div>- 3 → one dominant phenotype for one trait and one recessive phenotype for the other.</div><div>- 3 → one dominant phenotype for one trait and one recessive phenotype for the other.<br></div><div>- 1 → both recessive phenotypes.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-735845483b600d407e375c49efe587a51732d97d.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In a dihybrid cross with complete dominance, two <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>hetero</u><u>zygous</u> <u>(</u>PpTt x PpTt<u>)</u></span></u>&nbsp;parents produce offspring with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">9</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;ratio of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;phenotypes&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[phenotypes/genotypes]</span>."	"In a dihybrid cross with complete dominance, two <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>hetero</u><u>zygous</u> <u>(</u>PpTt x PpTt<u>)</u></span></u>&nbsp;parents produce offspring with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">9</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;ratio of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>phenotypes</b></span>.

This is the term for the updated version of Darwin's theory.</div>"
"In evolution, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;punctuated&#x20;equilibrium&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> refers to how populations experience <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>very little</i> evolution</span> for long stretches of time, followed then by a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">major burst in evolution</span>.&nbsp;"	"In evolution, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>punctuated equilibrium</b></span> refers to how populations experience <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>very little</i> evolution</span> for long stretches of time, followed then by a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">major burst in evolution</span>.&nbsp;

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

If they can't have offspring together, they are <u>not</u> the same species.</div>"
"<div>In speciation, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;reproductive&amp;nbsp&#x3B;isolation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when parts of a population become <u style="""">physically separated</u> and evolutionarily diverge apart - <i>losing</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">the ability to interbreed</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>In speciation, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>reproductive&nbsp;isolation</b></span> is when parts of a population become <u style="""">physically separated</u> and evolutionarily diverge apart - <i>losing</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">the ability to interbreed</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Because they diverged in their evolution and are no longer able to interbreed, they're <u>different species</u>.</div></div>"
"<div>In speciation, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>reproductive&nbsp;isolation</b></span> is when parts of a population become <u style="""">physically separated</u> and evolutionarily diverge apart - <i>losing</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;ability&#x20;to&#x20;interbreed"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>In speciation, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>reproductive&nbsp;isolation</b></span> is when parts of a population become <u style="""">physically separated</u> and evolutionarily diverge apart - <i>losing</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">the ability to interbreed</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Because they diverged in their evolution and are no longer able to interbreed, they're <u>different species</u>.</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Pre&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><u>zygotic</u> mechanisms of <b>reproductive isolation</b> are mechanisms that <i>prevent</i> formation of the zygote from happening."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>Pre</u></span><u>zygotic</u> mechanisms of <b>reproductive isolation</b> are mechanisms that <i>prevent</i> formation of the zygote from happening.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: breeding at different times, living in different niches, etc.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2652e13b24e75f49998b6746a202d223f586485c.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Post&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><u>zygotic</u> mechanisms of <b>reproductive isolation</b> are mechanisms that result in&nbsp;non-viable or sterile offspring."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>Post</u></span><u>zygotic</u> mechanisms of <b>reproductive isolation</b> are mechanisms that result in&nbsp;non-viable or sterile offspring.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: <b>hybrid organisms</b> can't produce viable offspring due to their uneven number of chromosomes.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2652e13b24e75f49998b6746a202d223f586485c.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Divergent&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>evolution</b> is when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">closely</span>-related species evolve <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>different</i>&nbsp;phenotypes</span> over time."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Divergent</b></span> <b>evolution</b> is when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">closely</span>-related species evolve <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>different</i>&nbsp;phenotypes</span> over time.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-7fc7b3f58fb027c4872e2725507e9d507ff69fcf.jpg""></div>"
"Starting from a common ancestor, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;parallel&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>evolution</b> is when two lineagaes split apart and form <u>similar characteristics</u>&nbsp;due to having&nbsp;<i>similar</i> environments."	"Starting from a common ancestor, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>parallel</b></span> <b>evolution</b> is when two lineagaes split apart and form <u>similar characteristics</u>&nbsp;due to having&nbsp;<i>similar</i> environments.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-7fc7b3f58fb027c4872e2725507e9d507ff69fcf.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Convergent&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>evolution</b> is when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">distantly</span>-related species evolve <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>similar</i> phenotypes</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Convergent</b></span> <b>evolution</b> is when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">distantly</span>-related species evolve <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>similar</i> phenotypes</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-7fc7b3f58fb027c4872e2725507e9d507ff69fcf.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-514a28887faa1dff90964b9984e17f6caa29e08b.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>molecular clock model</b></span> says that the <i>closer</i> the <u>genomes</u>&nbsp;are of two different species, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&#x20;recently&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[earlier/more recently]</span> they split apart in their evolutionary histories."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>molecular clock model</b></span> says that the <i>closer</i> the <u>genomes</u>&nbsp;are of two different species, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>more recently</i></span> they split apart in their evolutionary histories.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-0da7193e0d258b3240556766c3971a987822e4dc.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>molecular clock model</b></span> says that the <i>more different</i>&nbsp;the <u>genomes</u> of two different species, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;earlier&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[earlier/more recently]</span> they split apart in their evolutionary histories.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>molecular clock model</b></span> says that the <i>more different</i>&nbsp;the <u>genomes</u> of two different species, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>earlier</i></span> they split apart in their evolutionary histories.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Had more time to acquire dissimilar mutations.</div><div>- Called the <b>molecular clock model</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-0da7193e0d258b3240556766c3971a987822e4dc.jpg""><br></div>"
"In a dihybrid cross with complete dominance, two <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;hetero&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;u&gt;zygous&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&#x28;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;PpTt&#x20;x&#x20;PpTt&lt;u&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[heteroz./homoz.<u>]</span></u>&nbsp;parents produce offspring with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">9</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;ratio of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>phenotypes</b></span>."	"In a dihybrid cross with complete dominance, two <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>hetero</u><u>zygous</u> <u>(</u>PpTt x PpTt<u>)</u></span></u>&nbsp;parents produce offspring with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">9</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span>:<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;ratio of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>phenotypes</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 9 → dominant phenotypes for both traits.</div><div>- 3 → one dominant phenotype for one trait and one recessive phenotype for the other.</div><div>- 3 → one dominant phenotype for one trait and one recessive phenotype for the other.<br></div><div>- 1 → both recessive phenotypes.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-735845483b600d407e375c49efe587a51732d97d.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"CO<sub>2</sub> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;is&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[is/is not]</span> <u>soluble</u> in the bloodstream."	"CO<sub>2</sub> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>is</i></span> <u>soluble</u> in the bloodstream.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The disks themselves do not contain any bacteria. "In the <u>renin-angiotensin system</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;angiotensinogen&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is <i>first</i> synthesized in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span>."	"In the <u>renin-angiotensin system</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>angiotensinogen</b></span> is <i>first</i> synthesized in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Angiotensinogen is then <u>cleaved into</u> angiotensin 1 by <b>renin</b> (from the juxtoglomerular cells of the kidneys).<br><br><div><img src=""paste-1366e7507c6a8047868449ad03b0ce6bbc7f1803.jpg""></div></div>"
"In the <u>renin-angiotensin system</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>angiotensinogen</b></span> is <i>first</i> synthesized in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""liver"" data-ordinal=""2"">[organ]</span>."	"In the <u>renin-angiotensin system</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>angiotensinogen</b></span> is <i>first</i> synthesized in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Angiotensinogen is then <u>cleaved into</u> angiotensin 1 by <b>renin</b> (from the juxtoglomerular cells of the kidneys).<br><br><div><img src=""paste-1366e7507c6a8047868449ad03b0ce6bbc7f1803.jpg""></div></div>"
"<div><b>Aldosterone's</b> secretion is <i>triggered</i> by either activation of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>renin-angiotensin system</u></span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>blood potassium</u><u>&nbsp;levels</u></span>.</div>"	"<div><b>Aldosterone's</b> secretion is <i>triggered</i> by either activation of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>renin-angiotensin system</u></span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>blood potassium</u><u>&nbsp;levels</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Aldosterone pulls Na<sup>+</sup> into the bloodstream, while putting K<sup>+</sup> and H<sup>+</sup> back into the urine.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-1366e7507c6a8047868449ad03b0ce6bbc7f1803.jpg""></div>"

<div>Higher pressure in the arteries is due to the temporary contraction of the ventricles.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8dc5b08163be0e4e2e1c37196ede2bcdeadd8cca.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Low"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> <u>arterial blood pressure</u> corresponds to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>diastole</b></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Low</span> <u>arterial blood pressure</u> corresponds to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>diastole</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Lower pressure in the arteries is due to the temporary relaxation of the ventricles.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8dc5b08163be0e4e2e1c37196ede2bcdeadd8cca.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Low</span> <u>arterial blood pressure</u> corresponds to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;diastole&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[systole/diastole]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Low</span> <u>arterial blood pressure</u> corresponds to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>diastole</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Lower pressure in the arteries is due to the temporary relaxation of the ventricles.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8dc5b08163be0e4e2e1c37196ede2bcdeadd8cca.jpg""></div>"
"The heart's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>cardiac output (CO)</b></span> is <i>equal to</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;flow&#x20;rate&#x20;&#x28;Q&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of the blood."	"The heart's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>cardiac output (CO)</b></span> is <i>equal to</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>flow rate (Q)</u></span> of the blood.

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>arterioles</b></span> are the <i>primary</i> source of <u>vascular resistance</u>, because they have the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;greatest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;pressure&#x20;drop&#x20;&#x28;ΔP&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>arterioles</b></span> are the <i>primary</i> source of <u>vascular resistance</u>, because they have the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greatest</i> pressure drop (ΔP)</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">
