<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Random allele loss on the Y chromosome is more likely to happen&nbsp;</div><div><b>- Allele loss</b> is the <u>total elimination</u> of a particlar allele from a population.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-deee43042140c8363a71732c9163525d3b95f6f0.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>penetrance</b></span> refers to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;proportion&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;of&#x20;individuals&#x20;in&#x20;a&#x20;&lt;u&gt;population&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>&nbsp;carrying&nbsp;a particular allele that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>expresses&nbsp;</i>its phenotype</span>."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>penetrance</b></span> refers to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>proportion </i>of individuals in a <u>population</u></span>&nbsp;carrying&nbsp;a particular allele that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>expresses&nbsp;</i>its phenotype</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Huntington's disease example...</div><div>- More than 40 sequence repeats: full penetrance (100% express the Huntington's phenotype).</div><div>- Less than 40 sequence repeats: high penetrance (75% show phenotype, 25% are unaffected).</div><div>- Only a few repeats: low penetrance (majority do not show symptoms).</div><div><br></div><img src=""d3e9f734d60b65c585ad6aec6f4b4220.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;GHRH&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[hormone]</span>&nbsp;is released by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">hypothalamus</span>, which <i>stimulates</i> the <u>anterior pituitary</u> to release <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>growth hormone</b></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>GHRH</b></span>&nbsp;is released by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">hypothalamus</span>, which <i>stimulates</i> the <u>anterior pituitary</u> to release <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>growth hormone</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<u>Growth hormone-releasing hormone</u>. Stimulates anterior pituitary to release growth hormone.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-728952cae52ae9525bba6c96ef7f79cbff970365.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>GHRH</b></span>&nbsp;is released by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">hypothalamus</span>, which <i>stimulates</i> the <u>anterior pituitary</u> to release <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;growth&#x20;hormone&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[hormone]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>GHRH</b></span>&nbsp;is released by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">hypothalamus</span>, which <i>stimulates</i> the <u>anterior pituitary</u> to release <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>growth hormone</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<u>Growth hormone-releasing hormone</u>. Stimulates anterior pituitary to release growth hormone.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-728952cae52ae9525bba6c96ef7f79cbff970365.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Viruses&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> undergo <u>genetic mutations</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>significantly more</i></span> than <u>humans</u> because they <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>lack</i> DNA repair mechanisms</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Viruses</b></span> undergo <u>genetic mutations</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>significantly more</i></span> than <u>humans</u> because they <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>lack</i> DNA repair mechanisms</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

A mutation in a virus is more likely to occur than a mutation in a human.</div>"

"The <u>attachment</u> of <b>myosin</b> to <b>actin</b> in muscle contractions <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> require ATP - it <i>only</i> requires <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Ca&lt;sup&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;to bind to troponin to pull tropomyosin out of the way."	"The <u>attachment</u> of <b>myosin</b> to <b>actin</b> in muscle contractions <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> require ATP - it <i>only</i> requires <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Ca<sup>2+</sup></span>&nbsp;to bind to troponin to pull tropomyosin out of the way.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ca<sup>2+</sup>&nbsp;is moved back into the <b>sarcoplasmic reticulum</b> via a calcium-pump that requires ATP hydrolysis.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""0bc478aa81adc8c9d6ca0365f4d4bdb6.png""></div>"
"After a muscle contraction, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>reuptaken</i> back into</span>&nbsp;the <u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u> through a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;calcium&#x2D;pump&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> that is <i>powered</i> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ATP hydrolysis</span>."	"After a muscle contraction, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>reuptaken</i> back into</span>&nbsp;the <u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u> through a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>calcium-pump</b></span> that is <i>powered</i> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ATP hydrolysis</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>By contrast, Ca<sup>2+</sup> <u>does not</u> require ATP for its initial release out of the sarcoplasmic reticulum.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0bc478aa81adc8c9d6ca0365f4d4bdb6.png""><br></div></div>"

"After a muscle contraction, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>reuptaken</i> back into</span>&nbsp;the <u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u> through a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>calcium-pump</b></span> that is <i>powered</i> by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">ATP hydrolysis</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>By contrast, Ca<sup>2+</sup> <u>does not</u> require ATP for its initial release out of the sarcoplasmic reticulum.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0bc478aa81adc8c9d6ca0365f4d4bdb6.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In a muscle contraction, Ca<sup>2+</sup> <i>release</i> from the sarcoplasmic reticulum <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> require ATP.<br></div>"	"<div>In a muscle contraction, Ca<sup>2+</sup> <i>release</i> from the sarcoplasmic reticulum <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> require ATP.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>By contrast, ATP is required to pump Ca<sup>2+</sup> back into the sarcoplasmic reticulum after the contraction (since it moves against its concentration gradient).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0bc478aa81adc8c9d6ca0365f4d4bdb6.png""><br></div></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>ACh</b></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">hyper</span>polarizes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cardiac muscle</u></span>&nbsp;tissue in order to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>slow</i></span> its contractile rate.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Estrogen</b> thickens the endometrium while <b>progesterone</b> maintains it. When progesterone is low, nothing is able to stop the endometrium from shedding.</div><div>- Low estrogen &amp; progesterone levels at the beginning of the cycle is what triggers FSH &amp; LH production (stop negatively feeding back).</div><div><br></div><img src=""9154d3f6080f94150eca0ba38bca7b36.png""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>estrogen</b></span> is at its highest, it<i style="""">&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>positively</i></span> feedsback on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>GnRH</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>LH</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>FSH</u></span> - causing spikes in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""LH"" data-ordinal=""4"">[hormone]</span> to trigger <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ovulation"" data-ordinal=""4"">[process]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...happens at the very end of the <u><i>follicular phase</i></u>.</div><div>- Normally estrogen negatively feedsback on those hormones.</div><div>- Estrogen's positive feedback causes FSH, LH, and GnRH levels to rise - resulting in the spiking LH seen that causes <b>ovulation</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9154d3f6080f94150eca0ba38bca7b36.png""><br></div></div>"

"<b style="""">Artial natriuretic peptide (ANP)</b> is released by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">heart</u></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[lower/raise]</span> blood pressure."	"<b style="""">Artial natriuretic peptide (ANP)</b> is released by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">heart</u></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> blood pressure.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Puts Na<sup>+</sup> into the urine (water follows it). Results in decreased blood pressure.</div>"
"<b>Carbonic anhydrase</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> affect the <u>blood' pH</u> at equilibrium - it <i>only</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""speeds&#x20;the&#x20;rate&#x20;up"" data-ordinal=""1"">[function]</span>."	"<b>Carbonic anhydrase</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> affect the <u>blood' pH</u> at equilibrium - it <i>only</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">speeds the rate up</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Carbonic anhydrase</b> only speeds up the rate of the reaction - it <u>doesn't</u> change the equilibrium ratios of H<sup>+</sup>.</div><div>- Carbonic anhydrase catalyzes H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub> ⇌ CO<sub>2</sub> + H<sub>2</sub>O, since it ""removes"" the water molecule from carbonic acid (hence ""anhydrase"").</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-67b30148ac521094d3fc3bc6d2ca1f12dfe06cda.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Small</span> motor units are recruited <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;before&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[before/after]</span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">large</span> motor units."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Small</span> motor units are recruited <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>before</i></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">large</span> motor units.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Otherwise the muscule response would be <u style="""">too strong</u>.</div>"
"After a muscle contraction, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;reuptaken&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;back&#x20;into"" data-ordinal=""3"">[released from/reuptaken]</span>&nbsp;the <u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u> through a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>calcium-pump</b></span> that is <i>powered</i> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ATP hydrolysis</span>."	"After a muscle contraction, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>reuptaken</i> back into</span>&nbsp;the <u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u> through a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>calcium-pump</b></span> that is <i>powered</i> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ATP hydrolysis</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>By contrast, Ca<sup>2+</sup> <u>does not</u> require ATP for its initial release out of the sarcoplasmic reticulum.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0bc478aa81adc8c9d6ca0365f4d4bdb6.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Proteins"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> require <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>nuclear export sequences</b></span> to exit<i> </i>the <u>nucleus</u> <i>through</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">nuclear pores</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Proteins</span> require <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>nuclear export sequences</b></span> to exit<i> </i>the <u>nucleus</u> <i>through</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">nuclear pores</span>.

"Gram-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">negative</span> bacteria have <i>significantly</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>less</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;peptidoglycan&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[component]</span> in their membranes than gram-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive</span> bacteira."	"Gram-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">negative</span> bacteria have <i>significantly</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>less</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>peptidoglycan</b></span> in their membranes than gram-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive</span> bacteira.

"During <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>binary fission</b></span>, the cell's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>chromosomes</u></span> temproarily <i>attach</i> to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">plasma membrane</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Reductional division only happens from meiosis 1 (1° spermatocyte) → meiosis 2 (2° spermatocyte).</div><div>- 1 2° spermatocyte and 1 spermatid have the same amount of genetic information.</div><div><br></div><img src=""fd4c423b78cdbe70fc0742de0184ba16.jpg""></div>"
"1 <b>spermatid</b>&nbsp;has <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;the&#x20;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ratio]</span> amount of genetic information as a <u>2° spermatocyte</u>."	"1 <b>spermatid</b>&nbsp;has <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>the same</i></span> amount of genetic information as a <u>2° spermatocyte</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Reductional division only happens from meiosis 1 (1° spermatocyte) → meiosis 2 (2° spermatocyte).</div><div>- Meiosis 2 is equational division (same amount of genetic information).</div><div>- 1 2° spermatocyte and 1 spermatid have the same amount of genetic information.</div><div><br></div><img src=""fd4c423b78cdbe70fc0742de0184ba16.jpg""></div>"

"The <i>products</i> of myeloid &amp; lymphoid progenitor cells (mast cells, neutrophils, RBCs, etc.) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;do&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[do/do not]</span> <u>proliferate</u> in the bone marrow - they proliferate &amp; differentiate in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""their&#x20;respective&#x20;tissues"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <i>products</i> of myeloid &amp; lymphoid progenitor cells (mast cells, neutrophils, RBCs, etc.) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>do not</i></span> <u>proliferate</u> in the bone marrow - they proliferate &amp; differentiate in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">their respective tissues</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Myeloid &amp; lymphoid progenitor cells in the bone marrow are only sites of stem cell differentiation. The actual proliferation of their product cells occurs wherever those cells are needed.</div><div><br></div><img src=""57084bb16e9eb1d586a7ccfddc39a0b1.jpg""></div>"

Actual proliferation &amp; differentiation of the products of these stem cells <u>occurs in the tissues in which those cells normally reside</u>.</span></div></div>

"Can different <b>muscle fibers</b> within <i>the same</i> muscle produce <i>different</i> <u>contractile strengths</u>?<br><br><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Yes&#x2C;&#x20;muscle&#x20;fibers&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;same&#x20;muscle&#x20;&lt;i&gt;might&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;have&#x20;different&#x20;strengths&#x21;&#x20;Each&#x20;muscle&#x20;fiber&#x20;might&#x20;have&#x20;a&#x20;different&#x20;concentration&#x20;of&#x20;actin&#x2F;myosin&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"Can different <b>muscle fibers</b> within <i>the same</i> muscle produce <i>different</i> <u>contractile strengths</u>?<br><br><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Yes, muscle fibers in the same muscle <i>might</i> have different strengths! "<div>Different <b>muscle fibers</b> in <i>the same</i> <u>motor unit</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;can&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> produce different contractile strengths, since each might differ in their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""concentrations&#x20;of&#x20;actin&#x20;or&#x20;myosin"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>Different <b>muscle fibers</b> in <i>the same</i> <u>motor unit</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> produce different contractile strengths, since each might differ in their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">concentrations of actin or myosin</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The only thing you can guarentee is that each myofibril within the same muscle fiber contracts with the same strength.</div><div><br></div><img src=""796de507dfa0f6649582b38492e3a9b7.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Different <b>myofibrils</b>&nbsp;in <i>the same</i> <u>muscle fiber</u>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;cannot&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> produce different contractile strengths.</div>"	"<div>Different <b>myofibrils</b>&nbsp;in <i>the same</i> <u>muscle fiber</u>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> produce different contractile strengths.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Different muscle fibers within the same motor unit (or within the same muscle) <u>are</u> capable of having different contractile strengths though.</div><div><br></div><img src=""796de507dfa0f6649582b38492e3a9b7.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Bactericidal</b> <b>cells</b> are cells that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;kill&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;bacteria"" data-ordinal=""1"">[function]</span>."	"<b>Bactericidal</b> <b>cells</b> are cells that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>kill</i> bacteria</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: a macrophage that was both phagocytic &amp; bactericidal would exemplify the graph below after its intracellular bacterial cells are counted (declines over time because it kills the bacteria).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-a7e25c0346bb64a45b2b8cc245f9ea6548b7e0b2.jpg""><br></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">skeletal</span> muscle, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b>&nbsp;<b>channels</b> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>plasma membrane</u></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;mechanically&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-gated channels, while <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup> channels</b> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;voltage&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-gated channels."	"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">skeletal</span> muscle, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b>&nbsp;<b>channels</b> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>plasma membrane</u></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>mechanically</i></span>-gated channels, while <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup> channels</b> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>voltage</i></span>-gated channels.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Plasma membrane</b> is <u>mechanically gated</u> since Ca<sup>2+</sup> doesn't just enter the cell when stimulated by an A.P. - it needs to enter prior to excitation for storage in the S.R.<br></div><div>- <b>Sarcoplasmic reticulum</b> is <u>voltage-gated</u> since Ca<sup>2+</sup> only needs to be released when stimulated by the T-tubules' A.P.</div><div><br></div><div>In contrast, <b>cardiac muscle cells</b> <u>do</u> use voltage-gated Ca<sup>2+</sup> channels in their plasma membranes, since Ca<sup>2+</sup> influx is used to sustain the A.P. for coordinated contraction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""796de507dfa0f6649582b38492e3a9b7.jpg""></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">skeletal</span> muscle, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b>&nbsp;<b>channels</b> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;plasma&#x20;membrane&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[structure]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>mechanically</i></span>-gated channels, while <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup> channels</b> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;sarcoplasmic&#x20;reticulum&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[structure]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>voltage</i></span>-gated channels."	"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">skeletal</span> muscle, <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b>&nbsp;<b>channels</b> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>plasma membrane</u></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>mechanically</i></span>-gated channels, while <b>Ca<sup>2+</sup> channels</b> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>sarcoplasmic reticulum</u></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>voltage</i></span>-gated channels.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Smooth muscle cells are incredibly thin and therefore do not require this.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b2c84420062da553b25ee04ed4bcf5070ebe108d.jpg""></div>"
"Do <b>skeletal</b>, <b>smooth</b>, and <b>cardiac</b> muscle cells respond to <u>inhibitory</u> signals, <u>excitatory</u> signals, or <i>both</i>?<div><br></div><div>- Skeletal: responds to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;only&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;excitatory"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> signals.</div><div>- Smooth: responds to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;both&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;excitatory&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;inhibitory"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> signals.</div><div>- Cardiac: responds to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;both&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;excitatory&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;inhibitory"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> signals.</div>"	"Do <b>skeletal</b>, <b>smooth</b>, and <b>cardiac</b> muscle cells respond to <u>inhibitory</u> signals, <u>excitatory</u> signals, or <i>both</i>?<div><br></div><div>- Skeletal: responds to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>only</i> excitatory</span> signals.</div><div>- Smooth: responds to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>both</i> excitatory &amp; inhibitory</span> signals.</div><div>- Cardiac: responds to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>both</i>&nbsp;excitatory &amp; inhibitory</span> signals.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""3cf3d400e1263f33fa2f10336144f2c8.png""></div>"
"<div><b>Voltage-gated channels</b> found in <u>skeletal</u>, <u>smooth</u>, and <u>cardiac</u> muscle cells needed for the generation of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">action potentials</span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div>- Skeletal: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;primarily&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;Na&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x2D;gated"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br>- Smooth: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;primarily&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;Ca&lt;sup&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x2D;gated"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div>- Cardiac: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;both&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;Na&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x2D;gated&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;Ca&lt;sup&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x2D;gated"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><b>Voltage-gated channels</b> found in <u>skeletal</u>, <u>smooth</u>, and <u>cardiac</u> muscle cells needed for the generation of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">action potentials</span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div>- Skeletal: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>primarily</i> Na<sup>+</sup>-gated</span>.<br>- Smooth: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>primarily</i> Ca<sup>2+</sup>-gated</span>.</div><div>- Cardiac: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>both</i> Na<sup>+</sup>-gated &amp; Ca<sup>2+</sup>-gated</span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Na<sup>+</sup>-gated channels: fast-acting.</div><div>- Ca<sup>2+</sup>-gated channels: slow-acting.</div></div>"
"<div><b>Voltage-gated channels</b> found in <u>skeletal</u>, <u>smooth</u>, and <u>cardiac</u> muscle cells needed for the generation of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""action&#x20;potentials"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div>- Skeletal: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>primarily</i> Na<sup>+</sup>-gated</span>.<br>- Smooth: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>primarily</i> Ca<sup>2+</sup>-gated</span>.</div><div>- Cardiac: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>both</i> Na<sup>+</sup>-gated &amp; Ca<sup>2+</sup>-gated</span>.</div>"	"<div><b>Voltage-gated channels</b> found in <u>skeletal</u>, <u>smooth</u>, and <u>cardiac</u> muscle cells needed for the generation of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">action potentials</span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div>- Skeletal: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>primarily</i> Na<sup>+</sup>-gated</span>.<br>- Smooth: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>primarily</i> Ca<sup>2+</sup>-gated</span>.</div><div>- Cardiac: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>both</i> Na<sup>+</sup>-gated &amp; Ca<sup>2+</sup>-gated</span>.</div>

"<div>In <u>muscle tissue</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Na&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ion]</span><b>-gated channels</b> are for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>fast</i></span>-acting generation of A.P.s, while <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Ca&lt;sup&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ion]</span><b>-gated channels</b> are for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>slow</i></span>-acting generation of A.P.s.</div>"	"<div>In <u>muscle tissue</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Na<sup>+</sup></b></span><b>-gated channels</b> are for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>fast</i></span>-acting generation of A.P.s, while <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ca<sup>2+</sup></b></span><b>-gated channels</b> are for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>slow</i></span>-acting generation of A.P.s.</div>

<div>- Organic portion is primarily collagen.</div><div>- Made of calcium, phosphate, and hydroxide ions.</div><div><br></div><img src=""80e4b6c498b87f9e59069b14f5202dd9.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">Newborns</span>&nbsp;have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lower/higher]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>resting heart rates</u></span> than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">adults</span> in order to facilitate a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">greater amount of gas exchange</span> in the lungs."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">Newborns</span>&nbsp;have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>resting heart rates</u></span> than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">adults</span> in order to facilitate a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">greater amount of gas exchange</span> in the lungs.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>As we get older, our <b>resting heart rate</b> <u>decreases</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""3b8fa744ae75a01281c03a444600569e.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Recall that inhalation happens when the pressure in our lungs is lower than atmospheric pressure (flows down its gradient). When the two pressures are equal, <u>there's no influx of air</u> (hence what happens after your lungs have fully exhaled).<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fc134e66fa471dcf0ad311d40ceac5dda8f55c34.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Considered passive diffusion because it only ""opens the flood gates"" - water has to flow down its concentration gradient.</div><div><br></div><img src=""9c5dcd3a357421ac9129826cf1d7c881.png""><div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aquaporin</b></span> transports <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;water&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;across the plasma membrane through <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""passive"" data-ordinal=""2"">[active/facilitated/passive]</span> diffusion."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aquaporin</b></span> transports <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>water</u></span>&nbsp;across the plasma membrane through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">passive</span> diffusion.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Considered passive diffusion because it only ""opens the flood gates"" - water has to flow down its concentration gradient.</div><div><br></div><img src=""9c5dcd3a357421ac9129826cf1d7c881.png""><div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Aldosterone</b></span> pulls <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Na<sup style="""">+</sup></span><sup style="""">&nbsp;</sup><i>out</i> of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">distal convoluted tubule</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">collecting duct</u></span> of the nephron by <i>upregulating</i>&nbsp;the expression of the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Na&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x2D;K&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;ATPase"" data-ordinal=""4"">[enzyme]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Aldosterone</b></span> pulls <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Na<sup style="""">+</sup></span><sup style="""">&nbsp;</sup><i>out</i> of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">distal convoluted tubule</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">collecting duct</u></span> of the nephron by <i>upregulating</i>&nbsp;the expression of the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">Na<sup>+</sup>-K<sup>+</sup> ATPase</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Water follows the Na<sup>+</sup>, leading to an increase in blood pressure.</div><div><br></div><img src=""nephron1_med.jpeg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>mitochondria</b></span>&nbsp;in the midpeice of <u>sperm cells</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;degraded&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> after fertilization takes place since they're <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""tagged&#x20;with&#x20;&lt;u&gt;ubiquitin&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>mitochondria</b></span>&nbsp;in the midpeice of <u>sperm cells</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>degraded</i></span> after fertilization takes place since they're <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">tagged with <u>ubiquitin</u></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Anything that gets tagged with ubiquitin is destined for destruction by <b>proteosomes</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6eec98716264cc6c86c3897adee7c71f.png""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Too much genetic information is lost in the case of a monosomy.</div><div>- Trisomies can result in viable offspring though.</div><div><b><br></b></div><img src=""paste-0620d22b4cb8c3d87727d8263f4de7368ace9491.jpg""><br></div>"

"<div>When two nearby <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;transpons&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> face <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">the <i>same</i></span> direction, they align <i>parallel</i> to each other and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">undergo recombination</span> - resulting in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>deletion</u> of the DNA segments</span> that connected them.</div>"	"<div>When two nearby <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>transpons</b></span> face <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">the <i>same</i></span> direction, they align <i>parallel</i> to each other and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">undergo recombination</span> - resulting in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>deletion</u> of the DNA segments</span> that connected them.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 2 transposons are shown in blue below.</div><div>- Transposons that are near each other and point in the same direction can align with each other ni a parallel orientation - forcing the connecting DNA to loop around. <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 2 transposons are shown in blue below.</div><div>- Transposons that are near each other and point in the same direction can align with each other ni a parallel orientation - forcing the connecting DNA to loop around. After this, recombination occurs and the connecting DNA is deleted.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-1a6a0588332724f53604e7e55b2aaa5356a34e59.jpg""></div>"
"When two <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>transposons</b></span>&nbsp;are near each other in DNA and point in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>opposite</i></span> direction(s), they align in a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;parallel&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> orientation - resulting in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""a&#x20;sequence&#x20;inversion"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of the connecting DNA."	"When two <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>transposons</b></span>&nbsp;are near each other in DNA and point in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>opposite</i></span> direction(s), they align in a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>parallel</u></span> orientation - resulting in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">a sequence inversion</span> of the connecting DNA.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

When they both point in the same direction, they align parallel to each other and undergo recombination - resulting in the deletion of the gene.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-495f4885ce697e73cc0d04320cb7e36a4d282287.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"When two <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>transposons</b></span>&nbsp;are near each other in DNA and point in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;opposite&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[the same/opposite]</span> direction(s), they align in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>parallel</u></span> orientation - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">a sequence inversion</span> of the connecting DNA."	"When two <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>transposons</b></span>&nbsp;are near each other in DNA and point in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>opposite</i></span> direction(s), they align in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>parallel</u></span> orientation - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">a sequence inversion</span> of the connecting DNA.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Purkinje fibers then send the signals to the ventricles for contraction.</div><!--anki--><div><br></div><img src=""2451f38a906e227df5efda359f3f64c7.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Difference between the release of prolactin &amp; PIF:<br></div><div><br></div><div><b>PIF</b>: released by the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""hypothalamus"" data-ordinal=""1"">[structure]</span>.</div><div><b>Prolactin</b>: released by the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""anterior&#x20;pituitary"" data-ordinal=""1"">[structure]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>Difference between the release of prolactin &amp; PIF:<br></div><div><br></div><div><b>PIF</b>: released by the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">hypothalamus</span>.</div><div><b>Prolactin</b>: released by the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">anterior pituitary</span>.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Prolactin-inhibiting factor (same as&nbsp;<i>dopamine</i>)<div>- Apart of the&nbsp;<b>tuberoinfundibular pathway</b>&nbsp;(actuate nucleus → posterior pituitary)</div></div>"
"<div>When two nearby <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>transpons</b></span> face <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[the same/opposite]</span> direction, they align <i>parallel</i> to each other and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">undergo recombination</span> - resulting in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>deletion</u> of the DNA segments</span> that connected them.</div>"	"<div>When two nearby <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>transpons</b></span> face <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">the <i>same</i></span> direction, they align <i>parallel</i> to each other and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">undergo recombination</span> - resulting in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>deletion</u> of the DNA segments</span> that connected them.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 2 transposons are shown in blue below.</div><div>- Transposons that are near each other and point in the same direction can align with each other ni a parallel orientation - forcing the connecting DNA to loop around. After this, recombination occurs and the connecting DNA is deleted.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-1a6a0588332724f53604e7e55b2aaa5356a34e59.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Alveoli</b> that <i>lack</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;surfactant&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> require a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greater</i></span> amount of pressure to inflate the lungs in order to <i>overcome</i> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">water's surface tension</span> - <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increasing</i></span> the chance of lung collapse." "On a <b>Na<sup>+</sup>-gated channel</b>, a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>open</i>&nbsp;or <i>closed</i></span> <u>activation</u> gate &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closed</i></span> <u>inactivation</u> gate result in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">inactivated</span> state of the channel.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Both gates open → Na<sup>+</sup> flows inward.</div><div>- Activation open &amp; inactivation closed → inactivated channel.</div><div>- Activation closed &amp; inaactivation open → ""de-inactivated"" where it's not inactivated but not yet opened either.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""F1.medium.jpg""></div>"

"On a <b>Na<sup>+</sup>-gated channel</b>, a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;closed&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[open/closed]</span> <u>activation</u> gate &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;open&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[open/closed]</span> <u>inactivation</u> gate result in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">""de-inactivated""</span> state of the channel - it's not yet fully open."	"On a <b>Na<sup>+</sup>-gated channel</b>, a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closed</i></span> <u>activation</u> gate &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>open</i></span> <u>inactivation</u> gate result in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">""de-inactivated""</span> state of the channel - it's not yet fully open.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Both gates open → Na<sup>+</sup> flows inward.</div><div>- Activation open &amp; inactivation closed → inactivated channel.</div><div>- Activation closed &amp; inaactivation open → ""de-inactivated"" where it's not inactivated but not yet opened either.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""F1.medium.jpg""></div>"
"On a <b>Na<sup>+</sup>-gated channel</b>, a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closed</i></span> <u>activation</u> gate &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>open</i></span> <u>inactivation</u> gate result in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&quot;de&#x2D;inactivated&quot;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> state of the channel - it's not yet fully open." <script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<div>If the efferent arteriole was wider, blood would more easily pass into it, and the glomeruluar filtration rate would be lower.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-60bd3b7bef90aefee764de35379078a2a0055a46.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In the glomerulus, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>afferent</b></span> <b>arteriole</b> has a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;wider&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[more narrow/wider]</span> <u>diameter</u> than the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">efferent</span> arteriole, which helps to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>increase</i> the glomerular filtration rate</span>."	"In the glomerulus, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>afferent</b></span> <b>arteriole</b> has a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>wider</i></span> <u>diameter</u> than the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">efferent</span> arteriole, which helps to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>increase</i> the glomerular filtration rate</span>.

"<div>In neurons, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;myelin&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreases</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>capacitance</u></span> by keeping positive &amp; negative charges separated - resulting in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">voltage</span>.</div>"	"<div>In neurons, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>myelin</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreases</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>capacitance</u></span> by keeping positive &amp; negative charges separated - resulting in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">voltage</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Recall that <b>capacitance (C)</b> is inversely related to the separation of charges (distance). Since myelin separates the charges from each other, the capacitance is decreased and voltage is increased.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7bf590021238fd8bde556e627ccdc4c192315e6b.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""main-qimg-f36432e1f3ffd0ba5c16818ccfca645d.webp""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In neurons, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>myelin</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreases</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;capacitance&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[electrical property]</span> by keeping positive &amp; negative charges separated - resulting in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""voltage"" data-ordinal=""3"">[electrical property]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In neurons, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>myelin</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreases</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>capacitance</u></span> by keeping positive &amp; negative charges separated - resulting in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">voltage</span>.</div>

"In skeletal muscle, what initiates the <b>power stroke</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""The&#x20;&lt;u&gt;dissociation&#x20;of&#x20;ADP&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;P&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;sub&gt;i&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2E;&#x20;Do&#x20;&lt;i&gt;NOT&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;confuse&#x20;this&#x20;with&#x20;ATP&#x20;hydrolysis&#x2E;&#x20;ATP&#x20;hydrolysis&#x20;had&#x20;to&#x20;have&#x20;happened&#x20;&lt;i&gt;prior&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;the&#x20;initiation&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;power&#x20;stroke&#x20;in&#x20;order&#x20;to&#x20;&lt;i&gt;generate&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;ADP&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;P&lt;sub&gt;i&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;products&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"In skeletal muscle, what initiates the <b>power stroke</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">The <u>dissociation of ADP &amp; P</u><sub>i</sub>. "Three things that happen to <u>action potentials</u> when their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>K<sup>+</sup>-leak channels</b> are <i>blocked</i></span>:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Action&#x20;potential&#x20;duration&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;i&gt;increased&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Hyperpolarization&#x20;&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;occur&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Stimulates&#x20;&lt;i&gt;excess&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;contractions&#x20;in&#x20;muscles&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div>"	"Three things that happen to <u>action potentials</u> when their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>K<sup>+</sup>-leak channels</b> are <i>blocked</i></span>:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Action potential duration is <i>increased</i>.</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Hyperpolarization <i>does not</i> occur.</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Stimulates <i>excess</i> contractions in muscles.</span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Duration is increased because repolarization happens through the exiting of K<sup>+</sup> ions. <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Epithelial tissue only forms the internal lining of these organs.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-9ab0bfed417424f1ca717419c8f7632674a1fae0.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Epithelial</b></span> <b>tissue</b> cells protect <i>against</i> microbial infections by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;secreting&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;things&#x20;like&#x20;&lt;u&gt;mucus&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;sweat&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;and&#x20;&lt;u&gt;enzymes&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; providing a physicial barrier from the outside world."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Epithelial</b></span> <b>tissue</b> cells protect <i>against</i> microbial infections by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>secreting</i> things like <u>mucus</u>, <u>sweat</u>, and <u>enzymes</u></span> &amp; providing a physicial barrier from the outside world.
<hr>

"Why are <b>test crosses</b> <i>always</i> done between the <u>unknown</u> individual and a <i>known</i> <u>homozygous recessive</u> individual?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""If&#x20;a&#x20;trait&#x20;is&#x20;recessive&#x2C;&#x20;it&#x27;s&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;easy&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;tell&#x20;the&#x20;genotype&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;inidividual&#x20;based&#x20;on&#x20;their&#x20;recessive&#x20;phenotype&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;you&#x20;can&#x20;just&#x20;look&#x2E;&#x20;Crossing&#x20;them&#x20;with&#x20;a&#x20;wild&#x2D;type&#x20;individual&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;i&gt;less&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;reliable&#x20;though&#x2C;&#x20;because&#x20;you&#x20;&lt;i&gt;can&#x27;t&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;tell&#x20;their&#x20;genotype&#x20;just&#x20;by&#x20;looking&#x20;&#x28;i&#x2E;e&#x2E;&#x20;they&#x20;could&#x20;be&#x20;either&#x20;homozygous&#x20;or&#x20;heterozygous&#x20;for&#x20;the&#x20;dominant&#x20;trait&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Why are <b>test crosses</b> <i>always</i> done between the <u>unknown</u> individual and a <i>known</i> <u>homozygous recessive</u> individual?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">If a trait is recessive, it's <u><i>easy</i> to tell the genotype of the inidividual based on their recessive phenotype</u> - you can just look. Crossing them with a wild-type individual is <i>less</i> reliable though, because you <i>can't</i> tell their genotype just by looking (i.e. they could be either homozygous or heterozygous for the dominant trait).</span></div>

"<b>X-linked</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>recessive</i></span>&nbsp;traits are <i>more</i> likely to affect <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>males</u></span>.

"<b>X-linked</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;dominant&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[dominant/recessive]</span>&nbsp;traits are <i>more</i> likely to affect <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>females</u></span>."	"<b>X-linked</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>dominant</i></span>&nbsp;traits are <i>more</i> likely to affect <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>females</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since women inherit 2 X chromosomes, there's a higher likelihood that they'll get a mutated copy than a male, who only has 1 X chromosome.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9e39297a764ea669955ec051b811326f7aa75065.jpg""></div>"
"<b>X-linked</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>dominant</i></span>&nbsp;traits are <i>more</i> likely to affect <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;females&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[males/females]</span>."	"<b>X-linked</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>dominant</i></span>&nbsp;traits are <i>more</i> likely to affect <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>females</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since women inherit 2 X chromosomes, there's a higher likelihood that they'll get a mutated copy than a male, who only has 1 X chromosome.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9e39297a764ea669955ec051b811326f7aa75065.jpg""></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>uniform</i> expressivity</b></span> means that the patients with one particular genotype will manifest <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;exact&#x20;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;phenotype&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In genetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>uniform</i> expressivity</b></span> means that the patients with one particular genotype will manifest <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">the <i>exact same</i> <u>phenotype</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Uniform expressivity</b>: everyone with a particular genotype has the exact same phenotype.</div><div>- <b>Variable expressivity</b>: people with the same genotype might express the phenotype in various different ways.</div><div><br></div><img src=""d3e9f734d60b65c585ad6aec6f4b4220.png""><div></div></div>"
"In genetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;uniform&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;expressivity&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> means that the patients with one particular genotype will manifest <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">the <i>exact same</i> <u>phenotype</u></span>." "<b>Parasites</b> require the use of transporter proteins for proteins that are both <i>essential</i> to its survival &amp; are <i>normally</i> located in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cytoplasm&#x20;or&#x20;membrane&#x20;of&#x20;its&#x20;&lt;u&gt;host&#x20;cell&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[location]</span>." "<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Barr&#x20;bodies&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&#x28;inactivated&#x20;X&#x2D;chromosomes&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>do</i></span> <u>replicate</u>, but they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">one of the <i>last</i> chromosomes</span> to replicate." Pre-prohormone&nbsp;→ prohormone&nbsp;→ hormone</div>"
"Hormones derived from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">tyrosine</b></span> are synthesized in either the cell's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;cytoplasm&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;rough&#x20;ER&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"Hormones derived from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">tyrosine</b></span> are synthesized in either the cell's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">cytoplasm</u></span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">rough ER</u></span>.
